###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Ugly IF Statement

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T19:20:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AD7513E.8B12650B@home.com>`
- **References:** `<20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com> <3ad47463_1@anonymous> <3ad674e8_4@my.newsfeeds.com> <3AD688FF.3C5A2E62@home.com> <3ad7005b_4@my.newsfeeds.com>`

```


"Peter E. C. Dashwood" wrote:

Just re-read Michael's - can't disagree with a single thing he says.

> Some comments below, but no issues here. Please see also my>
> Are you suggesting that a pass is made to set the flags for the required
> extraction? More information please, Jimmy.
>

You refer to 'ASS-umptions" down below, but hell ! We've got to make some here,
as we only have the Ugly code to go on. (I don't hear the sound of somebody
cutting me a cheque for $160-200 per hour (US dollars please <G>))

So a 'real' example of the above :-

Firstly the 'old' record format :-

       01  VES-RECORD.
           05  VES-KEY.
               10  VES-KEY-NUMBER         PIC X(03).
               10  VES-RECORD-TYPE        PIC X(01).
               10  VES-SUFFIX                      PIC X(01).
           05  VES-IDENTITY-NUMBER    PIC X(10).
           05  VES-NAME                            PIC X(35).
           05  GROUP-TYPE REDEFINES VES-TYPE       PIC X.
           05  VES-REGISTRATION-NUMBER             PIC X(12).
           05  VES-FIRST-ITEM                      PIC 9(06).
           05  VES-LAST-ITEM                       PIC 9(06).

As you can see doesn't do much other than provide descriptive information, so I
didn't include a filler. And back then, not yet into three-tier systems, yours
truly had this format appearing in say some six programs or more. Along comes
user - "If we could 'squeeze' in the Vessel Type (Aerial Cooler, Line Heater,
Vessel with One Boot etc...), then you can produce me different calculations".

So choice, re-compile every damned program using the new format or try and do it
with one. Decided on latter so 'pinched' a character from the description as
follows :-

           05  VES-NAME                          PIC X(34).
           05  VES-TYPE                            PIC X.

This is Eileen's problem times 'n' . Although maybe only some 30 - 50 of these
records per file, there are umpteen files because of Company/Oil-Plant
designation.
So gave user a 'translate' program changing from pic x(35) to pic x(34) and pic
x(1).
And he has to *remember* to do this translation before he attempts to use a set
of files - not a very satisfactory solution - but given a twenty year old
compiler, the best I could come up with.

Now given the same situation with a 'modern' compiler, using F/J or N/E file
CBL_FILE DateTime stamp access, I can get at individual files to check. Still
got to do this conversion, but in my case, I determine the current set of files
being used at the Master Menu - so hard code a 'ChangeDate' into the Master Menu
and I can DateTime check, warning user :-

    - (1) I'm now going to convert your file (putting a space into the Type
code)
    - (2) Now edit the file for valid Vessel Types before processing

Still not foolproof - but better than the old way. There's no logic I can use to
insert his new Vessel Types so I just give him pic x value space. Two options
(1) Edit records individually through the Vessel Edit program, or (2) even
easier, produce an Ascill-delimited (CSV) which allows him to peruse quickly and
edit that one field for all records.

Hope that describes it sufficiently.

>
> Hey, we're not going to revisit the absorbing discussion on File Opens Vs
> Reads again are we...I'll just find my book of Vogon poetry...

Most definitely NOT -  Begin, Open, Process, Close, Finish.

On this one - Judson - I could sit down and write my own (i.e. do my own
homework <G>), but do you have any useful examples or timings showing the
dramatic difference between Open, Process ALL, Close - and - Open, Process this
one, Close, Open, Process this one, Close etc.... ?

> I saw Richard's suggestion re file flags and liked it. Liked even more his
> final summation:
> >
> Watch the verb "to assume", Jimmy. You know the old saying....
>

See above re 'assume'

More assumptions, or guesses. Eileen has just given us the one snippet (Ugly
code), but is this :-

    (a) A General Sales Analysis - if so

        if    NotaSpecialCustomer
              perform Standard-Routine

        else perform Exception-Routine

    (b) Special Customer Sales Analysis - i.e. it is only being done for a few
(?)
    selected accounts - then there might be justification for hard coding a
table -
    as the addition of type codes might only affect 1% of the data files.

Eileen - put us out of our agony - some input please <G>

Jimmy
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Ugly IF Statement

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T09:46:13+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AD80E55.9210D7C@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com> <3ad47463_1@anonymous> <3ad674e8_4@my.newsfeeds.com> <3AD688FF.3C5A2E62@home.com>`

```
> "Peter E. C. Dashwood" wrote:
> 
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> > go away and it won't get re-written (well, not overnight , anyway).

The ugly IF statement appears to be a _programmer's_ solution to the
requirement that some customers/classes/types need to be dealt with in a
special way.  A _designer's_ solution would be to build the conditions
into the database so that they can be changed without recompiling the
programs.  It seems to me that this may have started as a single case
and the analysts/designers passed it down to the coders as too trivial
for proper treatment.  

It has now grown and is not only ugly but is completely untestable.  How
much data is required to test that IF statement for working correctly ? 
How much is actually tested ?  Where is the specification that the code
can be compared to ?

While existing ugly code will still exist, no one will go back and
rewrite it because it may break something, there is no reason to accept
that it is acceptable practice for code in the future.  By making
programmers aware that these cases should be dealt with at the design
level (by introducing special case flags or set of data items) they
should be able to pass the requirement back up to the design level for
review _before_ the code gets ugly.

While it won't go away, there is no need to add more.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Ugly IF Statement

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T11:59:58+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2TBB6.49$Yi2.39705@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com> <3ad47463_1@anonymous> <3ad674e8_4@my.newsfeeds.com>`

```
Peter E. C. Dashwood <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam> wrote in message
news:3ad674e8_4@my.newsfeeds.com...
> I gave some more thought to this...
>
> While most of the thread is wailing about what awful code it is, and
> speculating about how it came to be and what would be a much better
solution
> (all fine, as far as it goes...) it occurred to me that there is a TON of
> this stuff sitting around on mainframes (and PCs probably...) and it won't
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> 1. Does anybody know of any source manipulation packages which can do
this?
>
> 2. Does anybody know of any potential sites where such a tool could be
sold?
> (I guess Eileen's would be one...<G>)
>
> 3. Anybody prepared to help write it, as a spare time back burner project
> with the only return being a share in the profits (if any)?
>

Well, Pete, software designed to create software has been with us since the
'50s, but methinks you have missed something of significance.

Who the hell wrote the "business rule" this software is supposed to support?
Twenty bucks ($US, $NZ or whatever) sez that twenty (quality) minutes with
the business decisionmaker results in better code. Probably would result in
a code something like

EVALUATE TRUE
   WHEN Designated-class
   WHen designated customer
   when designated location-code
     perform xxx
END-EVALUATE

where the "designated" class and customer are tabled lists.

Five bucks (not quite as confident) sez the 'location code'  qualifiers
could come out, too. That looks like old temporary code which was installed
prior to the designated class or customer being assigned to the customer.

I have no idea who the user is or even the industry, but I've seen enough
code like this. Sure looks like, " we'll just hard-code this and fix it
later." Of course, "later" never comes.

Cheap and Dirty gets the programmer home for dinner tonight. Doing it right
gets him home for dinner many more nights, after doing something far more
productive during the day.

What would be more interesting to develop would be some way of trapping how
many times a particular portion of this "rule" is actually invoked. For
example, are there any customers still assigned class 14? Is customer #29876
bankrupt and out of business?

Just as businesses set up periodic reviews of marketing plans, finance
plans, transportation plans, etc, IT managers should schedule periodic
reviews of business logic in code. Unfortunately,  too many managers have a
congenital inability to see beyond the next profit-and-loss statement to
make this small investment.

My $0.02 ($US)

MCM
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Ugly IF Statement

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T04:02:34+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ad727b0_2@my.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com> <3ad47463_1@anonymous> <3ad674e8_4@my.newsfeeds.com> <2TBB6.49$Yi2.39705@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
Hmmmm....

I agree of course, Michael and your money is safe <G>

I had limited the solution (quite deliberately) to the the constraints that
it must NOT introduce ANYTHING new...that means flags on files,
walkthroughs, meetings with the Business, or anything OTHER than the
EXISTING SOURCE CODE.

Certainly, better solutions are attainable and you are quite right to
question the business rules and who formulated them and when. I have
certainly been in this game long enough to know that, and have even
advocated it quite recently here in CLC  for the resolution of a Database
problem.

No, the fact remains that we do not live in a perfect World. Sometimes ALL
we have is the code. The people who wrote (perpetrated ?) it may be long
gone, business rules have been added to it like Topsy without the time or
inclination or resources to understand it, or try and improve it. So the
"reality" is the code. My speculation was that a tool which could bring some
kind of order to this code MIGHT be useful...(I'm in no particular hurry to
write it but would certainly do so if there's a Market for it...(my stance
on Market Forces is a matter of public record...<G>))

Even if it could make the job of a dedicated maintenance programmer easier
it might be worthwhile. Suppose Eileen had only needed 10% of the time she
spent dealing with this mess... It has to be worthwhile I would've thought,
but I could be wrong. Maybe the Management decision is just "re-write it in
Java". But even that isn't always viable (just as well, really...).

I have no issue with you and what you suggest is certainly better; my only
reservation is that sometimes this is NOT possible...

I'd still appreciate answers to the 3 qustions if anyone is interested.

Pete.

(few more comments below.)
```

## Thread: cobol thoughts — 5 msgs

- **From:** deek40@aol.com (Deek40)

```
I dont know if this is just working too much on cobol or i am starting to get
this stuff, but i find myself sitting it bed at night thinking about cobol. 
Call me crazy but i sit there and think if i do this to my program what will
the output change to and also a bunch of other cobol related things.  I still
get the error messages but in my mind i at least now get a direction of the
flow of what is going on behind the scenes of the compiler and what my program
is actually doing.

Deek
```

```
Dreaming and/or thinking about your job is common.  Just be glad you have a job
which requires thinking.   If your job was plugging a doodad into a thingamajig as
it passed you on the assembly line - you would still dream of it!!

Authors often keep pads of paper next to their bed, to make sure they write down
ideas they get in the middle of the night.   Sometimes programmers get up, dial
into work, and make changes as they think of them - but pads of paper would
probably work too - without using up sleep time.
```

```
> Dreaming and/or thinking about your job is common.  Just be glad you have
a job
> which requires thinking.   If your job was plugging a doodad into a
thingamajig as
> it passed you on the assembly line - you would still dream of it!!
>
> Authors often keep pads of paper next to their bed, to make sure they
write down
> ideas they get in the middle of the night.   Sometimes programmers get up,
dial
> into work, and make changes as they think of them - but pads of paper
would
> probably work too - without using up sleep time.
>
```

```
Any chance that we could take discussions of "energy policy" off-line or out
of this newsgroup?

If you feel a REAL need to comment/respond some more, would you PLEASE make
certain that your subject line starts:

  OT:

(This newsgroup is NOT moderated - but is targeted at COBOL and
COBOL-related issues.)
```

#### ↳ Re: "Administrative" request

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-11T17:26:38-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9b2ia2$c47$1@news.hitter.net>`
- **References:** `<9b27fg$sot$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Except, do not use a colon. Some news readers replace
initial characters, up to the first colon, with a reply indicator.
This means, for example, that "OT:" becomes "Re:", thus
losing that part of the subject line that shows the post  is
off-topic.
------------------
Rick Smith
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol compiler under Linux

- **From:** David Essex <dessex@arvotek.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T02:54:21-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AD6A29D.907344D6@arvotek.net>`
- **References:** `<3ad4c650$0$192$456d72a3@news.skynet.be> <02madtsp8hqcair6uqsonvrbrfhdn6msb7@4ax.com> <9b3ncm$p5a2@baran22.ttnet.net.tr>`

```
Onder OZ wrote:
> 
> Does TinyCOBOL run on SCO-UNIX as well? I Tried hard but couldn't succeed.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> i even couldn't compile the compiler :)

TinyCOBOL will run on IA32 platforms only.

The only currently supported platforms are Linux, FreeBSD, and Win32
(using the Cygwin emulation layer). 

There was an unconfirmed report that it ran on Solaris for IA32.

However since TinyCOBOL generates GNU assembler (ATT type), it may be
(and I stress MAYBE) possible to port it to run on SCO.


David Essex
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol compiler under Linux

- **From:** "Charles F. Townsend" <ctowns@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T06:00:18-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```

(Merant), AcuCobol(AcuCorp) and PERCobol(LegacyJ).  I a little partial to the
LegacyJ PERCobol compiler.

Charles Townsend
LegacyJ
```

```
I use COBOL because the type of programming I do requires COBOL --
that's
what my employers use.

Anyway, COBOL is a workhorse.  The functionality is there, the syntax is
readily grasped, and you aren't going to spend a lot of time debugging
syntactical errors.  I respect COBOL, and I can have fun with it at
times.
But my employers are paying me for timely code maintenance and
enhancement, 
that is, production.  

My two cents.
```

## Thread: AcuCobol: Copy - Paste an marked string with key — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### AcuCobol: Copy - Paste an marked string with key

- **From:** "David Neidinger" <d.neidinger@muffenrohr.de>
- **Date:** 2001-04-12T09:41:49+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9b3maj$o7t$03$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
Hello,

i have problems to put the copy - paste function to <strg>+<c> and
<strg>+<v>.

In the manual i found something but i it will not work.

it should work over "SET EXCEPTION".
Have anyone an example-code to put this function in my programm?

David Neidinger


----------------------------------------------------------------
Muffenrohr GmbH
Gï¿½terstr. 13
77833 Ottersweier

Tel.: 07223 2803-45
Fax.: 07223 2803-88
```

#### ↳ Re: AcuCobol: Copy - Paste an marked string with key

- **From:** "Erlend Moen" <erlend.moen@disys.no>
- **Date:** 2001-04-17T09:21:31+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bgqsr$3eb$1@oslo-nntp.eunet.no>`
- **References:** `<9b3maj$o7t$03$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
David Neidinger <d.neidinger@muffenrohr.de> skrev i
news:9b3maj$o7t$03$1@news.t-online.com
>
> i have problems to put the copy - paste function to <strg>+<c> and
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
The code looks like this in our system:

           SET     ENVIRONMENT      "KEYSTROKE" TO
                                    "Exception=31  ^C".
           SET     ENVIRONMENT      "KEYSTROKE" TO
                                    "Exception=32  ^V".
           SET     ENVIRONMENT      "KEYSTROKE" TO
                                    "Exception=33  ^X".
           SET     ENVIRONMENT      "KEYSTROKE" TO
                                    "Exception=34  ^Z".
           SET     EXCEPTION        31 TO COPY-SELECTION.
           SET     EXCEPTION        32 TO PASTE-SELECTION.
           SET     EXCEPTION        33 TO CUT-SELECTION.
           SET     EXCEPTION        34 TO UNDO.

The first four statements overrides the default exception-values because we
have already assigned for instance the F1-key to exception 3 - the same
value that Ctrl-C give us. Beside of that the four last statements is the
trick to make it work.

Good luck!

Erlend Moen
Norway
```

### Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

- **From:** Onder OZ <onderoz@MailAndNews.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-12T04:33:41-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADE7D10@MailAndNews.com>`

```
Hi,
here's my *first* program.

000010  IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
000011  PROGRAM-ID. DENEME.
000021  AUTHOR. ONDER OZ.
000022  ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
000023  CONFIGURATION SECTION.
000024  SPECIAL-NAMES. DECIMAL-POINT IS COMMA.
000031  DATA DIVISION.
000051  WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
000052  01 AA PIC X.
000061  PROCEDURE DIVISION.
000071          DISPLAY "Hello World!" AT LINE 1 COLUMN 1.
000081          ACCEPT AA.
000091          EXIT PROGRAM.

the problem is, DISPLAY statement goes to CONSOLE, and ACCEPT to SCREEN.
Please help!! :(

Onder OZ
```

#### ↳ Re: Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T07:59:31+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AD6A3D3.D4703FEE@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3ADE7D10@MailAndNews.com>`

```
Onder OZ wrote:
> 
> 000061  PROCEDURE DIVISION.
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> Please help!! :(

Actually it is the other way around.

Standard DISPLAY and ACCEPT default to being UPON CONSOLE, so your
ACCEPT is from the console.  If positioning phrases are added, such as
AT, then they default to being UPON CRT, so DISPLAY AT is on the screen.

The solution is to use all with the same variants, for screen change
your ACCEPT to be:  ACCEPT AA AT somewhere, or use FROM CRT or add
CONSOLE IS CRT in SPECIAL-NAMES.

From memory ACCEPT AT in Fujitsu requires every character to be filled
so you may need to space out to the end of AA.

If you want it all to be console then remove the AT from the DISPLAY.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2001-04-20T12:17:02+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae02838.120232641@news1.attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<3ADE7D10@MailAndNews.com> <3AD6A3D3.D4703FEE@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
On Fri, 13 Apr 2001 07:59:31 +0100, Richard Plinston
<riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote:


>
>Standard DISPLAY and ACCEPT default to being UPON CONSOLE, so your
>ACCEPT is from the console.  If positioning phrases are added, such as
>AT, then they default to being UPON CRT, so DISPLAY AT is on the screen.
>

This isn't quite correct - at least in the environments I have worked
in.  

DISPLAY in VMS send the display to the users terminal session, not the
console.

DISPLAY on any IBM mainframe writes to the printer.

With Fujitsu the Display is actually in the "user window" (so was the
accept).

To get "proper", or should I say "Accepted" behavior from Fujitsu v3
with Display ... Accept... you need to use UPON/FROM Console, else the
entry must be FULL in order to terminate the accept.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T12:30:36+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE16F5C.E957F7CA@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3ADE7D10@MailAndNews.com> <3AD6A3D3.D4703FEE@Azonic.co.nz> <3ae02838.120232641@news1.attglobal.net>`

```
Thane Hubbell wrote:
> >Standard DISPLAY and ACCEPT default to being UPON CONSOLE, so your
> >ACCEPT is from the console.  If positioning phrases are added, such as
> >AT, then they default to being UPON CRT, so DISPLAY AT is on the screen.

> This isn't quite correct - at least in the environments I have worked
> in.

This was specifically replying about Fujitsu (see subject header).

> DISPLAY in VMS send the display to the users terminal session, not the
> console.
> DISPLAY on any IBM mainframe writes to the printer.

Different systems interpret what the 'console' is in different ways.

> With Fujitsu the Display is actually in the "user window" (so was the
> accept).

If I use Fujitsu (4) and, without special-names or compiler options, do:

      DISPLAY "Unadorned"
      DISPLAY "upon console"  UPON CONSOLE
      ACCEPT input-field

these all come out in the same window that has a title: 'Console:
program-name'.
Thus, as I said, "standard DISPLAY and ACCEPT default to being UPON
CONSOLE".

Strictly speaking the default is SYSIN and SYSOUT, but these default to
being the Console: window.

When I use AT or UPON CRT or use SCREEN SECTION then these come out in a
window that has a title 'Screen: program-name'.  If this is also called
the 'user window' then this naming is merely confusing in giving it yet
a third name (CRT and/or Screen being the obvious names). In fact I
can't find any reference to the term 'user window' in either version 3
or 4 Fujitsu Cobol manuals.

> (so was the accept).

The behaviour described in the problem was that the display and accept
were in _different_ windows.  The use of 'AT' in the DISPLAY made it use
the 'Screen:', the ACCEPT defaulted to 'Console:'.  Your claiming that
they both actually came out in the same 'user window' doesn't seem to
help really.

> To get "proper", or should I say "Accepted" behavior from Fujitsu v3
> with Display ... Accept... you need to use UPON/FROM Console, else the
> entry must be FULL in order to terminate the accept.

One can use CONSOLE or SCREEN as required but it is best not to mix
these (as the problem program did).  MF Cobol (and others) use the same
window for both, Fujitsu uses separate windows for completely unknown
reasons.

Using UPON/FROM CONSOLE means not using positioning or attribute on the
DISPLAY/ACCEPTs.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

```
Yah - but when you said "Standard" I thought you were speaking in
general.

>
>      DISPLAY "Unadorned"
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>CONSOLE".

Without loading v3, which was different - I can't say for sure if this
was true of that version or not... however..

>When I use AT or UPON CRT or use SCREEN SECTION then these come out in a
>window that has a title 'Screen: program-name'.  If this is also called
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>help really.

I think you are right here and I was wrong.  There's only one fact
that leaves me puzzled.

When one does ACCEPT whatever....

If one is not in the "User Window" by virtue of a screen section, one
must fill in the entire contents of the field in order to terminate
the accept.  However, if one uses ACCEPT  whatever FROM CONSOLE the
accept terminates immediately.  

I was not answering the asked question - obviously and mistakenly -
your remarks are correct.  Thanks.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-22T19:41:51+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE325EF.604C8B95@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3ADE7D10@MailAndNews.com> <3AD6A3D3.D4703FEE@Azonic.co.nz> <3ae02838.120232641@news1.attglobal.net>`

```
Thane Hubbell wrote:

> Yah - but when you said "Standard" I thought you were speaking in
> general.

Cobol 'Standard' as distinct from X-Open.

> >these all come out in the same window that has a title: 'Console:
> 
> Without loading v3, which was different - I can't say for sure if this
> was true of that version or not... however..

Looking at the version 3 manuals the windows are named 'Console:' and
'Screen:', same as version 4.

> When one does ACCEPT whatever....
> 
> If one is not in the "User Window" by virtue of a screen section, one

I am not sure what you mean by "User Window", it is not a term used in
Fujitsu manuals and the context is ambiguous, it could be screen or
console depending on whether it is parsed as:

    not ( in the "user window" by virtue of a screen section ) 
 or ( not in the "user window" ) by virtue of a screen section 

And, of course, screen section isn't the only determinent of whether the
screen or console is used.

I think that you mean 'Console' when you say "User Window", but this
doesn't agree with your previous usage of this term:

> > With Fujitsu the Display is actually in the "user window" 

as the DISPLAY in question had 'AT' then it was to the Screen.

Which is it ? and why do you use this term ?


> must fill in the entire contents of the field in order to terminate
> the accept.  

I seem to recall that happened in version 3.0, it doesn't in 4.  

> However, if one uses ACCEPT  whatever FROM CONSOLE the
> accept terminates immediately.  

Hopefully not 'immediately', it should wait for an Enter (or other
nominated) key.  ;-)

ACCEPT FROM CONSOLE does have the annoyance that the number of
keystrokes are not constrained by the accepting field, one can type a
whole line for a single character input field.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help me please!!! (Fujitsu COBOL 3) - Program

```
On Thu, 12 Apr 2001 04:33:41 -0400, Onder OZ <onderoz@MailAndNews.com>
wrote:

>Hi,
>here's my *first* program.
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Hi,

I wrote a short program that maybe usefull for you.
It is written in Fujitsi cobol. It should do what you want.

Greetz,
rick
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
	PROGRAM-ID RICK.
	AUTHOR RICK DENKERS.
	ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
	CONFIGURATION SECTION.
	SPECIAL-NAMES.
		CRT STATUS IS KEYBOARD-STATUS.
	DATA DIVISION.
	WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
	01 KEYBOARD-STATUS.
		03 ACCEPT-STATUS PIC 9.
		03 FUNCTION-KEY PIC X. 
		 	88 F1-PRESSED VALUE X"01".
		03 SYSTEM-USE PIC X.

	01 SCREEN-ITEMS.
		03 AA PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
  SCREEN SECTION.
	01 NAME-ENTRY-SCREEN
		BLANK SCREEN, AUTO.
*
	              03 SCREEN-LITERAL-GROUP.
		     05 LINE 05 COLUMN 05 VALUE "HELLO WORLD !".
		      05 LINE 22 COLUMN 05 VALUE " PRESS F1 TO EXIT".

		03 REVERSE-GROUP REVERSE-VIDEO.
			05 LINE 06 COLUMN 05 PIC X(10) USING AA.
*
	PROCEDURE DIVISION.
	RICK-START.
		PERFORM DISPLAY-AND-ACCEPT-SCREEN UNTIL F1-PRESSED
   	STOP RUN.
*
	DISPLAY-AND-ACCEPT-SCREEN.
		DISPLAY NAME-ENTRY-SCREEN
		ACCEPT NAME-ENTRY-SCREEN.
```

```
I use a program written in Acucobol with microsoft OS

I use runtime 5.1

We have 4 pc, 2 with win98, 2 with windows 2000 professional

If install arc into windows 2000 professional, when i run program within
other pc (with over 2 pc) I note decrease performance when i open form (5-7
second for open form).
When form is loaded the program run with normal performance (5-7 second for
open file????)

If install arc into windows 98, the system run with normal perfomance with
client windows 98 or windows 2000

If install arc into windows 2000 server, the system run eith normal
perfomarce.

The problem is only when the arc is in windows 2000 professional

I have tried use the disk ntfs or fat32


Thank you
```

```
You have the option of writing the name of the opening menu in a Form; but
you can change it just by moving the name of another menu to "MenubarName"
of the specified Form at runtime.

regards,
```

## Thread: flexus Q's — 3 msgs

### flexus Q's

- **From:** "Gazaloo" <gaz@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T01:56:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<41tB6.12153$ix4.9625343@news1.rdc1.sfba.home.com>`

```
Bob, some quick (hopefully not too dumb) Q's:

1. last sp2 core release was 95?

2. can you "import" Micro Focus Dialog System screen sets?

3. is the connection for thin/web client secure?

4. does thin/web client require the cobol application to use SP2 for screen
i/o?

Gazaloo.
```

#### ↳ Re: flexus Q's

- **From:** bob7536@aol.com (Bob7536)
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T15:07:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010414110715.25340.00000160@ng-cq1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<41tB6.12153$ix4.9625343@news1.rdc1.sfba.home.com>`

```
Good Morning,

I can help you out with question number 1. I am using Flexus Sp2 version 4.0.31
which is the current release of the product.  This was last updated on
11/20/2000.  If you have not been updated since 1995 I believe you will see
some huge differences.  The interface has change alot from version 3 to 4. 
But, the one thing that has not changed is the product is rock solid.

Bob Hennessey
```

#### ↳ Re: flexus Q's

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 2001-04-18T21:10:47+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3addf17a.14939222@news.epix.net>`
- **References:** `<41tB6.12153$ix4.9625343@news1.rdc1.sfba.home.com>`

```
"Gazaloo" <gaz@home.com> wrote:

>Bob, some quick (hopefully not too dumb) Q's:
>
>1. last sp2 core release was 95?

Gazaloo:

Your questions are good ones...

The last major release for sp2 was version 4.0 which was released in
March of 2000.  Since then, we have had a few maintenance releases to
add some neat new enhancements and correct some problems.  The current
production release is version 4.0.31.

Since we first released the sp2 product back in 1986, we have always
provided regular major updates to the product on an annual (or even
more frequent) basis.  Our first Windows version was released in 1990,
so we've had a ton of experience in this environment.

>2. can you "import" Micro Focus Dialog System screen sets?

Sorry, the answer to this one is no.  But there is absolutely no
reason why one couldn't be written....even by one of our customers.
You would have to have some way of importing the screen set into a
COBOL program.  If Dialog has some sort of export tool which exports
the screen set definition to an external file, then it shouldn't be a
problem.

We can import a lot of different types of screen definitions, because
sp2 includes special Panel Maintenance Functions which can be invoked
directly from a COBOL program.  This allows the COBOL programmer to
completely build a screen definition "on the fly" from their COBOL
application.

These functions are in the standard off-the-shelf product and most of
the conversion programs available have actually been written by our
customers.

>3. is the connection for thin/web client secure?

Yep.  We provide a 128 bit data encryption routine (even the keys are
encrypted) which can be turned off or turned on by the COBOL
programmer.

In addition, we're adding authentication routines to the server side
processing right now which will help verify the identity of the client
machine when Thin Client first launches an instance of the COBOL
program.

>4. does thin/web client require the cobol application to use SP2 for screen
>i/o?

Yes.  The Thin Client is tailored to sp2, because the COBOL program
passes a very small parameter block to sp2 in order to display a
screen (or print a form remotely if you want to use our FormPrint
product with Thin Client).  Because the parameter block is so small,
the speed of Thin Client across a TCP/IP network is very fast.  It
would make less sense to create a generic Thin Client, because
Microsoft already sells one....Terminal Server.

In fact, sp2 and FormPrint both work with Terminal Server.  There
really isn't any need for our Thin Client if you use Microsoft
Terminal Server.  

But there are some disadvantages with Terminal Server.  One
disadvantage is that you cannot run an Active X Control on the client
machine and perform local client machine tasks.

With the Flexus Thin Client and COBOL sp2, you can actually utilize an
Active X Control to perform functions at the remote client site.  This
could include such tasks as com port I/O for point of sale terminals,
reading scales or other client side I/O tasks.

The other issue is that there is a serious limit to the number of
concurrent clients which can be supported using Microsoft Terminal
Server.  Terminal Server has an upper limit of a relatively low number
of concurrent users...Thin Client can support hundreds of concurrent
users in an NT environment.

The overhead associated with Terminal Server is huge.  Thin Client's
overhead is actually very small.

Another advantage of Thin Client is the fact that we support both the
UNIX and Windows 32 bit Server platforms.  Many of our customers
prefer using a UNIX server with a Windows 32 bit client as their
client/server platform of choice.  Terminal Server only works with
Windows.

I hope that I was able to assist you with your questions.


Bob Wolfe, flexus
When replying by e-mail, make sure that you correct the e-mail address.
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

```
me because I have gone over the code MANY times...about 1 hour's worth...I
am just horrible in COBOL...basically my problem is that the
'date-page-line' is currently NOT displaying on the screen, and I have no
idea why! Please help me, I know it's something stupid on my part :(

Antoine daccache

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------------------------------------------------------------

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. RECORD-DISPLAY.



       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.


       01 DATE-PAGE-LINE.
           10  FILLER                   PIC X(6) VALUE "Date:".
           10  RUN-MONTH-DISPLAY-VALUE  PIC X(10).
           10  RUN-DAY-DISPLAY-VALUE    PIC Z9.
           10  FILLER                   PIC X VALUE "/".
           10  RUN-YEAR-DISPLAY-VALUE   PIC 9(4).
           10  FILLER                   PIC X(50).
           10  FILLER                   PIC X(6) VALUE "Page:".

       01 CUSTOMER-SALES-REPORT-LINE.
           10  FILLER                   PIC X(33).
           10  FILLER                   PIC X(47)
                        VALUE "Customer Sales Report".

       01 TEMP-VALUES.
           10 RUN-DATE.
              15 RUN-YEAR          PIC 99.
              15 RUN-MONTH         PIC 99.
              15 RUN-DAY           PIC 99.

           10 CENTURY              PIC 9(4) VALUE 2000.

           10  RUN-MONTH-TABLE     OCCURS 12 TIMES.
               15  JAN                   PIC X(8) VALUE "January".
               15  FEB                   PIC X(9) VALUE "February".
               15  MAR                   PIC X(6) VALUE "March".
               15  APR                   PIC X(6) VALUE "April".
               15  MAY                   PIC X(4) VALUE "May".
               15  JUN                   PIC X(5) VALUE "June".
               15  JUL                   PIC X(5) VALUE "July".
               15  AUG                   PIC X(7) VALUE "August".
               15  SEP                   PIC X(10) VALUE "September".
               15  OCT                   PIC X(8) VALUE "October".
               15  NOV                   PIC X(9) VALUE "November".
               15  DEC                   PIC X(9) VALUE "December".


       PROCEDURE DIVISION.

       000-RECORD-DISPLAY.
           PERFORM 100-INITIALIZE
           PERFORM 200-DISPLAY-HEADINGS
           STOP RUN.

       100-INITIALIZE.
           ACCEPT RUN-DATE FROM DATE
           MOVE RUN-MONTH-TABLE(RUN-DAY) TO RUN-MONTH-DISPLAY-VALUE
           MOVE RUN-DAY TO RUN-DAY-DISPLAY-VALUE
           MOVE RUN-YEAR TO RUN-YEAR-DISPLAY-VALUE
           ADD CENTURY TO RUN-YEAR-DISPLAY-VALUE
           DISPLAY " ", ERASE.

       200-DISPLAY-HEADINGS.
           DISPLAY DATE-PAGE-LINE
           DISPLAY " "
           DISPLAY CUSTOMER-SALES-REPORT-LINE.
```

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>

```
You haven't told us which COBOL compiler you are using - so the following
may or may not work.  Some compilers have as an extension (not "standard")
the ability to specify where you want your display to show up.

Try for example changing your code to:

> > >        200-DISPLAY-HEADINGS.
> > >            DISPLAY DATE-PAGE-LINE
                          At Line 1 Column 1
> > >            DISPLAY " "
                          At Line 2 Column 1
> > >            DISPLAY CUSTOMER-SALES-REPORT-LINE
                          At Line 3 Column 1.
> > >
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Please help me!

- **From:** lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T20:58:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-3O2tdjKYYs3D@tcpserver>`
- **References:** `<9vuB6.90390$XV.23908100@news3.rdc1.on.home.com> <ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-KnZTVd9RXfk1@tcpserver> <%vIB6.1796$5E3.900066@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
The subscript you are using to obtain the transated month is "RUN-DAY"
If the day of the month is more than 12 you will get some arbitrary 
piece of 
binary data rather than the discription from your table. You should 
use
the run-month not the run-day

Also, the run-month-table is not constructed properly. The table
should be an element that occurs 12 times with all being the same 
size.
This table should "REDEFINE" a table of literals with the months in 
them.

10 table-of-literals
  15 filler pic x(10) value "january"
  15 filler pic x(10) value "february"
  15 filler pic x(10) value "march"
(etc etc)
10  run-month-table redefines table-of-literals occurs 12 pic x(10)

Lorne


On Fri, 13 Apr 2001 19:33:15, "Antoine Daccache" <oahfei@home.com>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Please help me!

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ( NA)
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T00:57:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<wfNB6.975$DW1.48592@iad-read.news.verio.net>`
- **References:** `<9vuB6.90390$XV.23908100@news3.rdc1.on.home.com> <ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-KnZTVd9RXfk1@tcpserver> <%vIB6.1796$5E3.900066@news3.rdc1.on.home.com> <ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-3O2tdjKYYs3D@tcpserver>`

```
In article <ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-3O2tdjKYYs3D@tcpserver>,

[snippage]

>Also, the run-month-table is not constructed properly. The table
>should be an element that occurs 12 times with all being the same 
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>10  run-month-table redefines table-of-literals occurs 12 pic x(10)

Hmmmmm... it may be a matter of style, of course, but I disagree; what
you've coded here would result in statements like

MOVE RUN-MONTH-TABLE (MO-NUM) TO HDR-LIN02-MO-LIT.

... or something like that.  The Decent, Good and True way to address this
would be:

10  MO-TBL.
    15  FILLER PIC X(10) VALUE 'JANUARY   '.
    15  FILLER PIC X(10) VALUE 'FEBRUARY  '.
et und cetera
10  MO-LIT REDEFINES MO-TBL OCCURS 12 PIC X(10).

... resulting in Honest, Proper and Maintainable code like:

MOVE MO-LIT (MO-NUM) TO HDR-LIN02-MO-LIT.

DD
```

```
The traditional way is something along the line of:

    DISPLAY SHOW-SOMETHING.
    DISPLAY "Type any key to continue ..." WITH NO ADVANCING.
    ACCEPT ANY-PIC-X.
    DISPLAY CLEAR-SCREEN-MY-COPYRIGHT.
    GOBACK.
```

- **From:** John H Sleight Jr <John_member@newsranger.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T01:32:04+00:00

- **Message-ID:** `<oMNB6.5897$FY5.448895@www.newsranger.com>`

```
Hi Antoine,

Someone mentioned you were using the wrong subscript to move the month text to
your display field. This could explain why the displays are misbehaving. If the
wrong values are moved to the text of the msg they can be interpreted as
terminal control characters and the system will attempt to execute them, causing
God knows what problems. 

Hope this helps, Jack.



In article <mbKB6.572347$Pm2.9244869@news20.bellglobal.com>, Donald Tees says...
>
>The traditional way is something along the line of:
…[123 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Please help me!

- **From:** "David de Jongh" <ddejongh@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-22T20:03:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cOGE6.30971$RF1.2593514@bgtnsc06-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<9vuB6.90390$XV.23908100@news3.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
You have defined your month table as occurring twelve times.  It should be a
redefines:
01 A.
  05 filler pic x(n) value 'first value'
  05 filler ....                 'second value'
etc.
01 table redefines A.
   05  month-entry   pic x(n) occurs 12 times.

Also, you are subscripting the month table with the day number, instead of
the month number.
Are you saying that the whole line displays as blanks, or that is just
displays the month incorrectly? YOu should be seeing the "Date:" string.
Also, why are you hard-coding the century?  Use ACCEPT DATE YYYYMMDD
instead.

D de J
```

## Thread: Help with logic? — 13 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help with logic?

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T18:12:36+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
I am trying to put together a generic logic tester. I am asking here
because, well, here is where the best logic guys are.

Given:
Three true/false conditions
Conditions may appear in any order
There is no "NOT" condition

Are the only possiblilities for compound conditions...

1 and 2  and 3      (all conditions true)
1  or  2   or   3      (any condition true)
(1 and 2) or  3       (either one condition true or both other conditions
true)

I think all other possible variations can be handled by rearranging which
conditions are number 1, number 2 and number 3.

But I could EASILY be wrong; this is not my strong suit.

Comments?
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with logic?

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald_tees@sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T18:43:58+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ONHB6.570830$Pm2.9226499@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
Three true/false is 000H thru 007H, eight conditions in all, is it not?
Anything generic is going to have to cover all eight, however you express
it.
01 variableA            PICTURE X VALUE "Y".
    88 FALSEA        VALUE "N".
    88 TRUEA        VALUE "Y".
etc.

If
    falsea and falseb and falsec
    perform do hex0-routine
else
if
    falsea and falseb and truec
    perform hex1-routine
else
*** etc.
else
if  truea and trueb and truec
    perform hex7-routine.

"Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net> wrote in message
news:okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net...
> I am trying to put together a generic logic tester. I am asking here
> because, well, here is where the best logic guys are.
…[36 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

```
I recently used code just like this.  It works good for 8 combinations but
it's fairly unusable for much anything bigger.

Is it possible to do this better with an EVALUATE or some other construct?

Jeff


> 01 variableA            PICTURE X VALUE "Y".
> 88 FALSEA        VALUE "N".
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
> perform hex.....
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help with logic?

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald_tees@sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T00:22:41+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<lLMB6.573223$Pm2.9261366@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net> <ONHB6.570830$Pm2.9226499@news20.bellglobal.com> <B6FD0E2D.8E7%jeffbaynard@home.com>`

```
sure. see below
<G>

"Jeff Baynard" <jeffbaynard@home.com> wrote in message
news:B6FD0E2D.8E7%jeffbaynard@home.com...
> I recently used code just like this.  It works good for 8 combinations but
> it's fairly unusable for much anything bigger.
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> > 88 TRUEA        VALUE "Y".
    01 THE-NUMBER        PICTURE 9.
> > etc.
> >
> > If
> > falsea and falseb and falsec
>>perform do hex0-routine
    MOVE ZERO TO MY-NUMBER
> > else
> > if
> > falsea and falseb and truec
> > perform hex1-routine
    MOVE 1 TO MY-NUMBER
> > else
> > *** etc.
> > else
> > if  truea and trueb and truec
> > perform hex.....
    MOVE 7 TO MY-NUMBER.
>

    EVALUATE MY-NUMBER
    etc.]

In all seriousness, you can treat each condition as a digit, but the worse
case is always going to be 2**x where x is the number of true/false
conditions.  In other words, you are simply replacing eight if statements
with eight elvaluations. The only way to shorten that is if a subset of the
conditions have a common routine.
```

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-13T19:41:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AD75630.3BE012F8@home.com>`
- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```


Michael Mattias wrote:

> I am trying to put together a generic logic tester. I am asking here
> because, well, here is where the best logic guys are.
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
> Comments?

OK Michael - Decision Tables :

---------------------------------------------
Conditions        Rules 1 through 8
----------------------------------------------
Red           Y    Y    Y    Y    N    N    N    N
White        Y    Y    N    N    Y    Y    N    N
Blue          Y    N    Y    N    Y     N   Y    N
---------------------------------------------
Actions (i.e. Perform Para 'x')
----------------------------------------------
Para A        x    -    -    -    -    -    -    -    -
Para B        -    x    -    -    -    -    -    -    -
Para C etc   -    -    x    -    -    -    -    -    -

-----------------------------------------------------

Conditions or Rules :

Start the bottom line with alternate Yes and No, double up on the next and so
on.
As you can see, too many Conditions and you'll go off the edge of the paper.
So when it gets this complex your actions are 'Go to Table xxx', i.e.
sub-divide the problem..

Actions : You determine those which are relevant and act accordingly. If it's
not Red, White, Blue either an error or ignore. Similarly some of the others
with a 'No' condition you may choose to ignore.

Jimmy
```

```
"James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com> wrote in message
news:3AD75630.3BE012F8@home.com...
>
>
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
> > (1 and 2) or  3       (either one condition true or both other
conditions
> > true)
> >
> > I think all other possible variations can be handled by rearranging
which
> > conditions are number 1, number 2 and number 3.
> >
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> ---------------------------------------------

If you look at this table edge on, you have a binary number that can be
handled by a case statement.  If you look at it vertically, and group
certain combinations with lower level logic, you have a generic if/then/else
chain. The choice of efficiency has to be made on the level of lower level
logic.  For example, can case x through y binary be assigned a single case
(all the instances of red or white). If it can, then you do not need an
exhuastive case statement, and can reduce the logic to an if/then sequence.




> Actions (i.e. Perform Para 'x')
> ----------------------------------------------
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> Start the bottom line with alternate Yes and No, double up on the next and
so
> on.
> As you can see, too many Conditions and you'll go off the edge of the
paper.
> So when it gets this complex your actions are 'Go to Table xxx', i.e.
> sub-divide the problem..
>
> Actions : You determine those which are relevant and act accordingly. If
it's
> not Red, White, Blue either an error or ignore. Similarly some of the
others
> with a 'No' condition you may choose to ignore.
>
> Jimmy
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with logic?

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T15:37:45+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ad7cc43_4@my.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```

Michael Mattias wrote in message ...
>I am trying to put together a generic logic tester. I am asking here
>because, well, here is where the best logic guys are.
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Michael,

>>>
Are the only possibilities for compound conditions...
<<<
Given that you are excluding NOT as an operator and only considering AND and
OR,  there are 4 ways in which you can combine 3 variables with 2 operators:
(& means "AND"; + means "OR")

A&B+C
A&B&C
A+B+C
A+B&C

You covered them, so the answer to your question is "YES".

The little universe described above has the properties which make it an
Algebra, (Association, Commutation, and Multiplicative and Additive
Identities (the Truth Values 1 and 0), even though we have not admitted
NEGATION to the Set.  If NEGATION was admitted, we could derive some further
Laws (named after a Frenchman, De Morgan) and it would then qualify as a
Boolean Algebra and we could do some useful work with it...<G>

There have been some very good posts regarding Decision Tables etc. but they
all deal with specific conditions. As has been pointed out, for three
specific variables (I think Jimmy used Red, Blue, and Green) there are 8
possible combinations. Don noted that you can view them in two different
ways and obtain "truth values". All excellent stuff. All easily
implementable in COBOL (Hey Guys! we're actually on topic for once...!)

There is another point you should consider...nested IFs.

<Begin Style War>

We have all seen horrendous examples of Nested IFs which make COBOL programs
almost unmaintainable.

Since the introduction of scope delimiters it has been ameliorated, but not
relieved entirely. Some installations ban them (like they ban anything you
might cut yourself with...ALTER, PERFORM...VARYING, INSPECT, SEARCH..
GO...TO,<G> (OK, I'm not entirely serious...but I have worked on
installations where PERFORM VARYING and SEARCH were banned; most people who
call themselves "Programmer" would find these pretty innocuous...).

Nested IFs are a useful and valid weapon in COBOL's armoury, especially when
used "properly"  I'm not going to get into a big discussion about Nested
IFs, but they are pertinent to Michael's enquiry for reasons I shall
describe.

I use Nested IFs ONLY to describe a decision TREE. I only "nest" in the TRUE
branches. Used in this way, Nested IFs cover a specific set of cases which
COULD be covered by a series of compound conditions, but it is arguably more
elegant to use a single construct.

The truth value for MY nested IFs can be described as:
(where ' represents Negation. read C' as NOT C, and => as 'implies')


A&B&C => Action1
A&B&C'=> Action2
A&B'      => Action3
A'           => Action4

In "English"...(Conditions represented by c1 c2 etc.)

if c1
    if c2
        if c3
             *> (A&B&C)
              Do Action 1
        else
             *> (A&B&C')
              Do Action 2
         end-if
     else
          *> (A&B')
           DoAction 3
     end-if
else
     *> A'
     Do Action 4
end-if

Your Logic evaluator needs to be aware of the equivalence between Nested IFs
and compound conditions, Michael. Remember that some of the above conditions
may well be compound ones including negation...

</end Style War>

Finally, the other excellent posts regarding Decision Tables may have left
some of the newcomers wondering HOW they might actually implement this in
code.

Apart from doing it by table lookup, here is a simple way for tables with
only a few conditions...
(Using the notation defined above...)

move zero to logic-value
if c1
   add 1 to logic-value
end-if
if c2
   add 2 to logic-value
end-if
if c3
   add 4 to logic-value
end-if
if c4
   add 8 to logic-value
end-if
evaluate logic-value
     when zero
              (c1+c2+c3+c4)'
     when 1
              c1&(c2+c3+c4)'
     when 2
               c2&(c1+c3+c4)'
     when 3
              c1&c2&(c3+c4)'
     when 4
              c3&(c1+c2+c4)'
     when 5
              c3&c1&(c2+c3)'
     when 6
              c3&c2&(c1+c4)'
     when 7
               c1&c2&c3&c4'
     when 8
               c4&(c1+c2+c3)'
     when 9
               c4&c1&(c2+c3)'
     when 10
               c4&c2&(c1+c3)'
     when 11
               c4&c2&c1&c3'
     when 12
               c4&c3&(c1+c2)'
     when 13
               c4&c3&c1&c2'
     when 14
               c4&c3&c2&c1'
     when 15
               C4&c3&c2&c1
end-evaluate

Newbie question: Why do you suppose there is NO "when...other" in this
evaluate? What does that mean? What would happen if one of the Actions taken
under a WHEN actually changed the value of the logic-value field?

Hope this helps (somebody...) <G>

Pete.






-----= Posted via Newsfeeds.Com, Uncensored Usenet News =-----
http://www.newsfeeds.com - The #1 Newsgroup Service in the World!
-----==  Over 80,000 Newsgroups - 16 Different Servers! =-----
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help with logic?

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T14:13:34+00:00

- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net> <3ad7cc43_4@my.newsfeeds.com>`

```
Peter E. C. Dashwood <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam> wrote in message
news:3ad7cc43_4@my.newsfeeds.com...
>
> Michael Mattias wrote in message ...
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

A direct answer. Thank you.

My goodness, what have I spawned? A huge style war?

All I was trying to do in my system is this:

You, Ms. User, may set from zero to three true/false conditions for an
action to occur.

When there are no conditions, I don't do nothing.
When there is one condition, I will test for true or false
When there are two conditions, I will test for both true, one true or none
true.
When there are three conditions, I will test for:
   1 and 2  and 3      (all conditions true)
   1  or  2   or   3      (any condition true)
   (1 and 2) or  3       (either one condition true or both other conditions
true)

You, Ms. User, can do any combination of truths within this definition,
although you may need to rearrange the order of your conditions to insure
the condition you want is tested.

Boolean Logic is not my strong suit, so I thought I'd just ask here. Sheesh,
I guess I should have listened to my mother when she told me to be careful
what I asked for.....

MCM
PS: But I kind of like that 8-way-box thing. Maybe I'll put in a user
maintainable truth table.. that would certainly let me add more
conditions...?????
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help with logic?

- **From:** Calvin Crumrine <Calvin_Crumrine@dced.state.ak.us>
- **Date:** 2001-04-16T13:56:57-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADB6AA9.B7B05CC8@dced.state.ak.us>`
- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net> <3ad7cc43_4@my.newsfeeds.com> <iWYB6.1065$dW5.124417@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
Michael Mattias wrote:

> Peter E. C. Dashwood <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam> wrote in message
> news:3ad7cc43_4@my.newsfeeds.com...
…[39 more quoted lines elided]…
> conditions...?????

As noted earlier, the 8-way box becomes unwieldy if you go much beyond 3
conditions. A different way combines the 2 approaches. You'd need to spend more
time analyzing it than I have to determine how efficient it is. This is just
real quick, off the top of my head, now.

Take 4 conditions, A, B, C, and D. You also have 4 tests.
1) a and b and c and d
2) a or b or c or d
Run these tests once with:
a=A, b=B, c=C, d=D

3) (a and b) or c or d
Run this test 7 times with:
a=A, b=B, c=C, d=D
a=A, b=C, c=B, d=D
a=A, b=D, c=B, d=C
a=B, b=C, c=A, d=D
a=B, b=D, c=A, d=C
a=C, b=D, c=A, d=B
(Note that the order of a and b don't matter, nor does the order of c and d.
That's what keeps this test to 7 iterations.)

4) (a and b and c) or d
Run this test 4 times with:
a=A, b=B, c=C, d=D
a=A, b=B, c=D, d=C
a=A, b=C, c=D, d=B
a=B, b=C, c=D, d=A

Now I believe that this conducts as many tests as the n-way box, but it might be
easier to manage, particularly for larger values of n. Plus, maybe you could
find an even easier way. For example, test the conditions 2 at a time. Test 4
then becomes ((a and b) and c) or d. You can use the 2-variable switching logic
from test 3 to cycle thru the first part. Or maybe this becomes so inefficient
code-wise that it overshadows the efficiency maintenance-wise. Like I said, I
haven't exactly analyzed this yet.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with logic?

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T17:40:21+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AD87D75.2D4A73D1@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<okHB6.1200$Yi2.275360@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
Michael Mattias wrote:
> 
> I am trying to put together a generic logic tester. I am asking here
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> There is no "NOT" condition

This is what Decision Tables were for.  

                        1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 E
     Condition 1        Y Y Y Y N N N N
     Condition 2        Y Y N N Y Y N N
     Condition 3        Y N Y N Y N Y N

8 possible, 2^3. With decision table processors the actions would then
be listed below this with an indicator as to which should be performed.
```

```
On the menu for each script, there's a 'File' choice. Below that is
'Print.' I don't think there's a way to print the whole program nor do
I think copy books are expanded in the print routine.

Copy books are not expanded during debugging in V5 (I keep copies of
copy books in a folder on my desk - we have about 100). I'm happy to
see that they are in Version 6.
```

```
I believe that you can get a complete listing by setting some of these options
COPY=Yes
PRINT=Yes <drive>:<path you want listings in>
SOURCE=Yes

Hope that this helps.
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu 6.0 compiler Listing

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)

```
In order to get full and complete listings from Fujitsu COBOL (not
PowerCOBOL) set all of the following compiler options.  Neglecting any
may result in listings with no copybooks or listings that are
incomplete:

COPY
PRINT
SOURCE

You will proably also need:

LIB and LIBEXT with appropriate settings for your installation.


On 14 Apr 2001 05:07:54 GMT, merhottin@aol.com (Merhottin) wrote:

>statements, and one of the bad things about Fujitsu, is the only time I see the
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
```

```
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------------------------------------------------
      IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. RECORD-DISPLAY.



       DATA DIVISION.

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       01 DATE-PAGE-LINE.
           10  FILLER                   PIC X(6) VALUE "Date:".

       01 TEMP-VALUES.
           10 RUN-DATE.
              15 RUN-YEAR          PIC 99.
              15 RUN-MONTH         PIC 99.
              15 RUN-DAY           PIC 99.

           10 CENTURY              PIC 9(4) VALUE 2000.

           10  TABLE-OF-LITERALS.
               15  JAN                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "January".
               15  FEB                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "February".
               15  MAR                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "March".
               15  APR                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "April".
               15  MAY                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "May".
               15  JUN                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "June".
               15  JUL                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "July".
               15  AUG                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "August".
               15  SEP                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "September".
               15  OCT                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "October".
               15  NOV                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "November".
               15  DEC                 PIC X(10)  VALUE "December".

                                OCCURS 12 PIC X(10).

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.

       000-RECORD-DISPLAY.
           PERFORM 100-INITIALIZE
           PERFORM 200-DISPLAY-HEADINGS
           STOP RUN.

           MOVE SPACES TO RUN-MONTH-DISPLAY-VALUE

           MOVE RUN-DAY TO RUN-DAY-DISPLAY-VALUE
           MOVE RUN-YEAR TO RUN-YEAR-DISPLAY-VALUE
           ADD CENTURY TO RUN-YEAR-DISPLAY-VALUE
```

<a id="t-c9b63332f4"></a>
## Thread: OT - BMC performance question — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Off-topic and spam`](topics.md#spam)_

### OT - BMC performance question

- **From:** "Rufio" <davecawdell@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-14T14:46:04+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MoZB6.2253$181.587876@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```
Hi.

Does anyone know the performance difference between INDEX BUILD (default)
and INDEX UPDATE, on BMC LOADPLUS, where a process loads 1 day's
data into a DB2 table of 6 months data (adding 0.6% to the volume)?

I've read the diagnostics for the LOAD phase, and it looks like the load of
the
rows constitutes less than 5%of the phase elapse time, with the rest taken
up by
the index unload/concatenation of 1 days new index entries/sort/reload.

Bearing in mind this is a 1.1 billion row table of 16 paritions, you can
start to see
the financial implications. I saw this on Thrusday, and knowing there is no
serial
processing on the table, just 2 transactions doing random access, I think I
may
be able to use INDEX UPDATE, with a weekly index rebuild and a monthly
REORG.

I also anticipate savings in the preload, if I can get LOADPLUS to stop
SORTing.
I do a SORT/split before I start the load job, creating files for each
partition load.

THX.
```

<a id="t-213438e561"></a>
## Thread: Help for program POO to Fujitsu 4 — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help for program POO to Fujitsu 4

- **From:** "Nancy" <nancy.jeunehomme@skynet.be>
- **Date:** 2001-04-15T20:26:29+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ad9e900$0$4459$456d72a3@news.skynet.be>`

```
I must write a Tree class which models the binary trees (not balanced) .
One needs these comparisons to be able to carry out the operations of
insertion, of research, of erase...
The only easy way is that the data to enter the tree are of the objects of
the Datatype type.  It is thus necessary to define what the relation "
smaller " between two objects of this type means. When can one say that
object 1 is smaller than the 2? It is when the entirety entier1 of object 1
is smaller than the entirety entier1 of object 2.
To speak to you, I use a translator, I hope that that can go on the level of
the translation.
Thanks for your help

A+

Nancy
```

## Thread: COBOL FAQ — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Meta: FAQs, group policy, charter`](topics.md#meta)_

### COBOL FAQ

- **From:** wmklein@ix.netcom.com (William M. Klein)
- **Date:** 2001-04-16T04:35:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,news.answers,comp.answers,alt.answers
- **Message-ID:** `<computer-lang/cobol-faq_987395367@rtfm.mit.edu>`

```
Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2000/07/02
Version: 3.01
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Sunday, July 2, 2000

The COBOL FAQ has moved.  To view the most recent version, please check out the
web version at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send private
email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

The following is Appendix B.5 - Changes to create Version 3.01

 o Recorded new location of the FAQ
 o Updated some old (bad) web links
 o Added some new COBOL books (particularly those written or contributed to - by
frequent comp.lang.cobol submitters).
 o Added information about LegacyJ (PERCobol) and changed the company name from
"Synkronix" to "LegacyJ".
 o Updated information for changes in product and company names and addresses
where available
 o Updated the URLs for downloading the draft of the next COBOL Standard and
CobCy
 o Started the Samples/Example Section
 o Tried to get rid of most specific references to Windows/NT, Windows/95,
Windows/98, and Windows/2000.
 o Added links to Xinotech
 o Added information on Norcom and GUI ScreenIO
 o Added (back) information on Wang
 o Added (back) information on the Tiny-COBOL ('74 Standard) project
```

<a id="t-32c44b9319"></a>
## Thread: E-Commerce — 3 msgs

### E-Commerce

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-16T17:35:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADB2D40.B9A350EF@home.com>`

```
I note Russell's message where he said topics seem to have dried up.
Well I was going to write this one anyway Russ <G>

Probably like most, I'm a bit jaundiced by all the hype about
e-commerce, and grin when I see all those yuppie dot coms going down the
tubes. However e-commerce does appear to have some merit - catalogue
your products on the Net and HOPE somebody will buy.

But here's an interesting twist happening in Calgary. Husky Oil has been
around for quite some time, most notably known for its gas stations but
recently took over Renaissance Energy one of the local 'darlings',
medium sized oil/gas exploration. The latter misstepped, so having grown
to a certain size, became a juicy candidate for takeover. (The takeover
sharks are in a frenzy in the oil patch and any 'loose change' available
in the States is also being used).

Anyway back to Husky - following (paraphrased) written by a computer
consultant (yuck!) in today's paper. Husky uses the SAP suite of
e-business applications to do admin and handle materials (inventory).
Husky implemented wireless PDA(Personal Digital Assistant) to record
accurately consumption of inventory at each warehouse.
They formerly used the traditional , purchase order, receive delivery,
write cheque etc. Under new approach each supplier is responsible for
ensuring products are always on hand at all Husky warehouses. Each
supplier issues a MONTHLY invoice based on CONSUMPTION - not shipments,
(so the latter bit means it is the supplier's problem if he tries to
shovel unwanted product into your warehouse).

System produces a DAILY receipt and consumption status for each
supplier, sent to suppliers via the Net who then decide what to ship.
(means Husky is not concerned with admin effort associated with managing
low value items). Husky uses UNIX on servers, and Windows CE 3.0 on its
PDAs. SAP uses Oracle DBMS and Visual Basic used to construct and
maintain application to run on PDA components.

Now of course the above doesn't cover all the specifics, and like any
application there is a downside. The writer of the article may well have
had a hand in the design - but he doesn't comment. Nor is there a
mention of the language used with SAP - could it be C or C++ ? (Don has
had dealings with SAP back East - any ideas on language ?).

Just so it doesn't appear as one 'glowing' story. On the downside
Renaissance shares were at one time around $50 but new Husky shares
stand at about $13. Husky is some 75% controlled by Li Kai Shang (not
correct spelling, but close), 5th richest gent after Billy Gates. Reason
for low shares - the investment community aren't too thrilled at 75% of
the action being controlled by one shareholder.

Anyway, anybody got any other interesting e-commerce stories, or neat
approaches to using the Net ?

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<z8GC6.678162$JT5.18044963@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<3ADB2D40.B9A350EF@home.com>`


<a id="t-306c286c10"></a>
## Thread: COBOL FAQ - frequency of "pointer" posting — 15 msgs

_Topics: [`Meta: FAQs, group policy, charter`](topics.md#meta)_

### COBOL FAQ - frequency of "pointer" posting

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-16T22:14:56-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bgcg6$sf9$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I just received the following "private" email from:
 CHARLESCRIBBS@prodigy.net

"why do you find it necessary to just clutter the world with stuff you
haven't checked in months?

if you want to post information that's one thing but just posting the same
thing over and over just clutters all the servers you send to.  get off you
lazy butt and put something up that people can use not just your web
address."

  ***

I believe that the last time the newsgroup discussed the FAQ, there was
general consensus that a "frequent" (multiple times per month) "pointer" to
the FAQ web page was the best thing to do with it.

Is there now any general opinion to change this - or should I just "ignore"
this specific comment?

OBVIOUSLY, I am always interested in specific input on changes to make.  I
have an "ongoing to do list" of changes - and will admit that it has been a
WHILE since my last update.  However, I don't think that any of the comments
that I have received (since the last update) really REQUIRES an update.

I know that we STILL get questions (in the NG) about "Where do I get a free
compiler?" and "What books are available on COBOL?" - both of which ARE
answered in the FAQ.

Furthermore, I do not actually do the posting every 10 days (or so).  Rather
it is the "FAQ news-server" that does it.  However, I would be more than
willing to modify its frequency or information - if there actually was
"general" input to do so.
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL FAQ - frequency of "pointer" posting

- **From:** Peter Lacey <lacey@mb.sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2001-04-16T22:32:10-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADBB939.6DA21AB2@mb.sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<9bgcg6$sf9$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```

> I just received the following "private" email from:
>  CHARLESCRIBBS@prodigy.net
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>

The service a frequent "pointer" provides are far more useful than the bad

specific comment.

PL
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL FAQ - frequency of "pointer" posting

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2001-04-17T11:49:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010417074912.29204.00000125@nso-cl.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<9bgcg6$sf9$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
In article <9bgcg6$sf9$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>, "William M. Klein"
<wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> writes:

>
>if you want to post information that's one thing but just posting the same
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Bill,
     Personally, I would chalk this up to the 'ignore' column.
After such a long discussion 'back when' you were looking for a 
solution to 'cluttering' the newsgroup with the entire FAQ, I feel that 
the brief pointer message provides a better service.  It's frequency
if not so far apart that new browsers will miss it or have trouble locating
a current reference on how to get there.
I may be a bit more callous with regard to this situation, but as with
most posts of this nature I take it with a  grain of salt and figure someone
had a bad day and needed to vent on someone.

The pointer to the FAQ is much easier to deal with in my newsreader
```

```
Terry Heinze wrote:

> Anyone who reads this newsgroup regularly knows that you're one of the more
> valuable contributors, Bill.  'Nuf said.

Bill,

As everybody says 'keep at it', it is a valuable service. However some thoughts
on trying to draw it to the attention of those that need to know :-

Newbies :

1. Where did you pick up on that the NewsGroup comp.lang.cobol existed ?
2. When posting a homework query, did you first search messages ?
3. If 'Yes' to # 2, were you aware of COBOL FAQ ?
4. If not aware of FAQ what 'catchy' title would draw your attention to it as a
newcomer, "Student Help', "Answers on COBOL" etc........

Others : (Books & Compilers) :

Were you aware of FAQ ?

National Languages :

Perhaps a link to appropriate country if a 'local' FAQ exists. Get this, using
Babelfish you could do a short message in Deutsch, French, Portuguese, Spanish,
Italian ? <G>

Just some thoughts - you might want to contact some of the recent newbies (Deek
etc.) and gauge their reactions. Similarly, what is Volker's reaction, and
perhaps contact some of the latest who submitted in French, Italian etc.

Jimmy
```

```
Ignore the comment.

Based on my news server - if I move to a new machine and "subscribe"
to c.l.c I get a download of x number of messages.  In most cases (and
recentlly I seem to be the computer crash king, so I move around a
lot) I get 1 copy of the FAQ with that download.

What that tells me is that people FIRST entering this group, who ARE
looking for help and have never seen the FAQ before will get a copy.
We obviously cannot quantify how many posts are NOT made because of
the FAQ, but I imagine it is significant.

Your frequency is fine.
```

#### ↳ Re: restructure-tool

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-17T13:24:27-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bi1pn$ce$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<9bhinl$kei$1@news.kdt.de>`

```
1) *PLEASE* tell me that you are not now doing an "OS/VS COBOL" ("cobol1")
to "VS COBOL II ("cobol2") migration at this time.  VS COBOL II is NO LONGER
supported by IBM - and is replaced by "IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM.

2) GO TO label-name is *fully* supported in:
   VS COBOL II (even though the compiler is now out of service)
    IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM (current IBM "strategic" COBOL product)
    draft of *next* COBOL ANSI/ISO Standard.

3) GO TO (without a "label-name") - i.e. GO TO used with ALTER is *not*
supported in the draft of the next COBOL Standard but *is* still supported
in IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM (and IBM has given no "serious" indication of
removing it in the foreseeable future).

4) If your shop WANTS to get rid of "GO TO" statements (because of "shop
standards" or ease of maintenance), I fully agree with this as a "good goal"
(although some may disagree - especially when it comes to GO TO
para-name-EXIT structures).  IBM *used* to market a product called
"COBOL/SF" (COBOL Structuring Facility).  I believe that it has (recently)
been withdrawn - but I am checking on that.  The same functionality was
available (on the PC) with their "Professional" version of VisualAge COBOL.
I will also check and see if that is still available or not.  There used to
be a NUMBER of 3rd party vendors with "restructuring" tools.  Most of them
were FAIRLY EXPENSIVE - and left your code at various degrees of
"un-maintainable" source code.

Others may have experience with some of the automated restructuring tools -
and can tell you if they have had much (any?) success with them.
```

- **Date:** 2001-04-18T04:56:56+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADD1E5E.B73FBA2C@worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<9bhinl$kei$1@news.kdt.de> <9bi1pn$ce$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
"William M. Klein" wrote:
> (snip)
> 4) If your shop WANTS to get rid of "GO TO" statements (because of "shop
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> and can tell you if they have had much (any?) success with them.

I used IBM CSF about 8 years ago, and even put several restructured
programs into production.  It's an interesting tool.  At that time I
had essentially no experience with TSO/ISPF, and it was set up to use
TSO panels to enter your configuration/restructuring parameters and to
submit a job to read old source code and generate new source code.  A
lot of the configuration options affected the appearance of the output
program (for example indent 3 columns or 4 columns on IF statements). 
The output program could be generated with only paragraphs or with all
sections (popular in Europe).  For really ugly Perform-thru's with
multiple entry points and exit points it generated working-storage
switches that controlled branching in the output program.

I never had an output program that wouldn't compile.  Most of the
programs I restructured had dead code that could not be generated to
the output program.  There's an option to include old paragraph names
as comments in the generated code, and to generate "called-by"
comments in each paragraph.  I found that restructured programs had
fewer paragraph-names than the original unstructured version.  Every
program I tried out appeared to work correctly after restructuring,
although I did not do full-blown regression tests.

IBM's COBOL Structuring facility was very good at eliminating GO TO's,
ALTER's, and PERFROM-THRU's, but I found the results a little
disappointing.  If the data-names and paragraph names were unclear in
the original program, they were still unclear in the generated
program.  I still found myself fixing up names, especially paragraph
names, in the generated program to make it more understandable.

They had the product for a few years, then dropped in a budget-cutting
move because I was the only applications programmer who ever learned
how to use it.  I still find old production programs that could
benefit from CSF.  If we still had the tool I would definitely use it
again, but the need doesn't arise very often.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: restructure-tool

```

LONGER
> supported by IBM - and is replaced by "IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM.

We are using VS COBOL II.

>
> 2) GO TO label-name is *fully* supported in:
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> standards" or ease of maintenance), I fully agree with this as a "good
goal"
> (although some may disagree - especially when it comes to GO TO
> para-name-EXIT structures).  IBM *used* to market a product called
> "COBOL/SF" (COBOL Structuring Facility).  I believe that it has (recently)
> been withdrawn - but I am checking on that.  The same functionality was
> available (on the PC) with their "Professional" version of VisualAge
COBOL.
> I will also check and see if that is still available or not.  There used
to
> be a NUMBER of 3rd party vendors with "restructuring" tools.  Most of them
> were FAIRLY EXPENSIVE - and left your code at various degrees of
> "un-maintainable" source code.

But the sourcecode is created by a generating tool. It generates go to
label-statements no cobol2-structures (with END-Statements). We want to work
without the program generating tool. But the generated code is not good to
maintain, so we look for a tool to restructure it.

>
> Others may have experience with some of the automated restructuring
tools -
> and can tell you if they have had much (any?) success with them.
>
…[15 more quoted lines elided]…
```
