```
Ralf
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: restructure-tool

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-18T15:42:58-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bku9k$e6p$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<9bhinl$kei$1@news.kdt.de> <9bi1pn$ce$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <9bjfnk$t1g$1@news.kdt.de>`

```
Please do look at when/if you are going to be migrating to a supported
compiler.  Are you at least using the LE run-time library for your VS COBOL
II programs?

Now, back to restructuring.

From my experience, moving from the "generated" COBOL code from application
generators to "native" COBOL code almost ALWAYS requires a real programmer
and not an "automated tool".

I know of such "migrations" that have been done from Telon, APS, and
Pacbase.  As far as I know, the ONLY successful migrations have been
"programmer done rewrites" - and not any type of "automated tool".  The
reason is simple,

ALL of these application generators are specifically created so that their
COBOL codes is *NOT* maintainable at the COBOL level but REQUIRE you to
maintain the application at the "higher level language" of the generator.
It is not a matter of (just) paragraph and data names, but it is the entire
way they are "structured" to follow a "template" programming module that is
NOT intended to be directly maintained by application programmers.

I would STRONGLY recommend that you get a gradual "migrate/upgrade/rewrite
when doing normal maintenance" strategy and NOT try and do any mass
migration.
```

## Thread: another new problem — 8 msgs

### another new problem

- **From:** "Onder OZ" <kucuk_calik@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-18T09:55:26+03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bja5d$b041@baran22.ttnet.net.tr>`

```
Hi,
After solving prior issues, i came to another problem that i couldn't solve.

LINKAGE SECTION.
01 PAKET-REC.
   02 A PIC X.
   02 B PIC X.
01 PAKFIR-REC.
   02 C PIC X.
   02 D PIC X.

PROCEDURE DIVISION USING PAKET-REC PAKFIR-REC.
PROC-START.
        DISPLAY A.
        DISPLAY B.
        END PROGRAM.

And the compilation generates following errors:

chs.cbl 107: JMN3399I-S  'A' OF DISPLAY STATEMENT MUST BE DEFINED IN
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
chs.cbl 107: JMN3399I-S  'B' OF DISPLAY STATEMENT MUST BE DEFINED IN
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.


How can i solve this???

Best regards & TY
Onder OZ
```

#### ↳ Re: another new problem

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T06:54:43-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADEE013.451EF7D@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<9bja5d$b041@baran22.ttnet.net.tr>`

```
Onder OZ wrote:

> Hi,
> After solving prior issues, i came to another problem that i couldn't solve.
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
> How can i solve this???

Interesting.  I wonder if that is particular to your compiler - I don't know if
I have ever tried to display something from the LINKAGE SECTION, I would guess
yes, but I don't know.

So the compiler is telling you that what it needs to display has to be in
WORKING-STORAGE, you have it in LINKAGE.   I would believe the compiler and copy
it to WORKING-STORAGE before displaying.
```

#### ↳ Re: another new problem

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T09:07:26-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<fiCD6.4102$rk3.167897@news1.atl>`
- **References:** `<9bja5d$b041@baran22.ttnet.net.tr>`

```
"Onder OZ" <kucuk_calik@yahoo.com> wrote:
> Hi,
> After solving prior issues, i came to another problem that i couldn't solve.
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
> WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

1. Check the CALL statement in the calling program to be sure the
   arguments are being passed for PAKET-REC and PAKFIR-REC, and that
   they are the correct size and type.

2. Check for a qualification problem in the called program.

3. Your compiler may simply not be able to DISPLAY from the Linkage
   Section, though that sounds peculiar.  I regularly use items in
   the Linkage Section for screen DISPLAY/ACCEPT in Net Express.
```

```
Hi,

In a mainframe environment, if you're accepting parm info from the exec JCL, you
need a parm len field - pic s9(004) comp. Also, it's better to code the LS:

01  lk-parm-info.
05  lk-parm-len pic     s9(004) comp.
05  lk-paket-rec.
10  lk-a        pic  x(001). 
10  lk-b        pic  x(001). 

05  lk-pakfir-rec.
10  lk-c        pic  x(001). 
10  lk-d        pic  x(001). 


In article <q9ND6.19939$L7.169553@news1.sshe1.sk.home.com>, mikman says...
>
>so why not just define to new variables in the workign storage and move A
…[40 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-20T06:53:05+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADFCEC1.AF5B8490@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<9bja5d$b041@baran22.ttnet.net.tr>`

```
Onder OZ wrote:
> 
> Hi,
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
>         END PROGRAM.
 
Ignoring your 'errors':

This should be a CALLed routine (it will fail if it is tun stand
alone).  What do you expect to happen after the displays ?  What do you
expect 'END PROGRAM' to do ?  What does the manual say that it does ?
```

#### ↳ Re: another new problem

- **From:** awkisall <awkisall@qwest.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T00:20:09-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE134A9.FF2C7815@qwest.net>`
- **References:** `<9bja5d$b041@baran22.ttnet.net.tr>`

```
Your compiler was unusually specific and to the point.

Move A to WS-DISPLAY-A
Move B to WS-DISPLAY-B

DISPLAY WS-DISPLAY-A
DISPLAY WS-DISPLAY-B.

predicated upon Working-Storage fields set up according to the properties of A
and B
```

<a id="t-0665a20c76"></a>
## Thread: Free VSAM Space Requirements Software — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Free VSAM Space Requirements Software

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-18T14:48:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ePhD6.142$756.60528@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`

```
April 18, 2001

This is a repost of something I posted about six months ago. Those who work
in the mainframe environment and are responsible for defining and allocating
VSAM datasets may find this of interest.

In approximately October, 1999, I asked if anyone could suggest a 'VSAM for
Dummies'- type tool to enable a calculation of the space allocations which
should be used during an IDCAMS DEFINE of a VSAM dataset. No one could, so I
decided to write one myself. Thanks to several people who helped me with
this project, that software is now available.

In October, 2000, I released Version 1.1 of "VSAMSpace." VSAMSpace is a
single 55K Windows(r) 32-bit (NT/9x/2K) executable program file used to
calculate the cylinder or track allocations required for various types of
VSAM datasets under various conditions.

The user selects the type of file (KSDS, RRDS or ESDS), the allocation unit
(cylinders or tracks), CISIZE, CI% and CA%, the record length and a couple
of other parameters, clicks the "Space" button and the software returns a
little message of the form:


ESDS on 3390
CISZ= 512  CI: 10 CA: 10
Var  LRECL= 200/400
Number of Records:   10,000
Requires      8  Cylinders

        OR

KSDS on 3390
CISZ= 512  CI: 15 CA: 10
Var  LRECL= 200/400 Key  24
Number of Records:    5,000
Requires     59  Tracks

Click the "Copy" button to copy this data to the Windows clipboard, and do
what you will with it. (This is how I got the output into this note). Change
parameters, click "Space" again and the space is recalculated; you can see
how CISIZE and other parameters affect your DASD space allocation
requirements.

When you click the "Doc" pushbutton, it extracts the doc file to a text file
in the same directory where the executable is and launches NOTEPAD to view
it. All the arithmetic of "how space is calculated" is in the documentation.

Operation of the software is so simple even a mainframer can handle it.

VSAMSpace does not require any support DLLs, does not read from or write to
the system registry or do any other annoying stuff. To install it, you "save
e-mail attachment." To run it, you click on it twice in Windows Explorer. To
un-install, you delete the file. (Although I doubt anyone would EVER
de-install it!).

And, the best thing about this software - at least insofar as I can tell
about the BBS/Usenet crowd - is that the price is right: free.

If you'd like a copy of this software, just send an e-mail request to
michael.mattias@gte.net . The request should have a valid "reply to" address
which I do not need to edit. I can attach the file to the email as an EXE or
as a ZIP file (about 25K) if you need to get past a firewall or just feel
better about it that way; so let me know if you you want the software
attached as "EXE" or "ZIP." (Default shipping format is ZIP format).

Note: For those who already have a copy of this software, this is NOT a new
version. You can check your version using the "properties" button against
your VsamSpace.exe file using Windows Explorer. The current version is
1.1.0.
```

#### ↳ Re: Free VSAM Space Requirements Software

- **From:** yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama)
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T03:16:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010418231617.06553.00000124@ng-fx1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<ePhD6.142$756.60528@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`

## Thread: Talking to NT MS SQL from HP3000 COBOL — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Talking to NT MS SQL from HP3000 COBOL

- **From:** mano@mano.com (freewheelin)
- **Date:** 2001-04-18T15:24:35+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3addb0bd.983232@news.uswest.net>`

```

I need some help talking to MS SQL server on NT from an HP3000.

I have a COBOL program that calls a C program with dblib calls and
writes to Sybase on Unix and Sybase on NT through this program.  This
is my first try talking to SQL Server 7 on NT.

Does anyone know why calling dblib on NT server for Sybase works but
doesn't work with MS SQL?  Is a different lib required?  The MS web
pages I've looked at turned up blanks.

PROGNAM error handler
 severity = 9
 dberr    = 2002
 oserr    = 68
 dberrstr = Read from SQL Server failed
 oserrstr = RECEIVED A GRACEFULL RELEASE OF THE CONNECTION. (SOCKERR
68)
Operating-system error: RECEIVED A GRACEFUL RELEASE OF THE CONNECTION.
(SOCKERR 68)
DB-Library: SQL Server connection failsed.

thks.

John
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujistu COBOL Error

- **From:** thaneH@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T01:16:51+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae0df17.44765707@news1.attglobal.net>`
- **References:** `<3ADE0F55.284A@epix.net>`

```
I suspect that this is an F3xxxxxx.DLL file and you can edit and
compile but cannot run your program.  USUALLY this is because the
Fujitsu installer with v3 has a difficult time amending the path
statement in your autoexec.bat or your autoexec.bat is not running.

Please tell us which OS you are running and we will try to help.

Bottom line, if you took the defaults on install, you need to get:

C:\FSC\PCOBOL32 into your path.


On Wed, 18 Apr 2001 19:04:05 GMT, Christine Geary <cgeary@epix.net>
wrote:

>Recently I downloaded Fujitsu COBOL Version 3.1 from their web site.  When I went to run 
>the program, I got an error message that there was a file or something that it couldn't 
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>Christine
```

<a id="t-37255fef0c"></a>
## Thread: STD - (US) Reaffirmation period ends next Monday — 6 msgs

### STD - (US) Reaffirmation period ends next Monday

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T08:55:13-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
The US Public Review period ends next Monday, April 23.  If you have any
comments about the "reaffirmation" of the US (not ISO) '85 Standard,
Intrinsic Function Module, or Correction Amendment,  NOW is the time to send
them to:

    Deborah Donovan, email: ddonovan@itic.org
        and
    ANSI, ATTN PSA Dept., email: psa@ansi.org

You do *NOT* need to be a US citizen to send comments on these.  You should
(in your comments) say that they relate to the reaffirmation of:

 Reaffirmation of a Standard.
    X3.23:1985 [R1991]: Programming Language COBOL

    X3.23a:1985/ADD 1:1989 [R1991]: Addendum to ANSI X3.23-1985, Programming
Language - Intrinsic Function Module for COBOL

    X3.23b:1985/ADD 2:1993 [R1998]: Title: Correction Amendment to ANSI
X3.23:1985, Programming Language COBOL and ANSI X3.23a-1989 Addendum 1

  ***

If you are interested in the currently submitted comments, you may look at
them at:
 http://www.ncits.org/archive/2001/it01200r/it01200r.htm

 ***

My *personal* (and this is NOT a very popular opinion) hope is that some
more commentors will indicate that:

A) J4 should spend its time and resources on "finishing up" the revision of
the Standard (and doing work on its planned "technical reports") and *not*
take up (again) the burden of "maintaining" these current COBOL standards -
that are certainly "functionally frozen" and that I think those vendors that
do support - will continue to support whether or not there is an ANSI
Standard.

B) The "perception" about COBOL is *not* impacted ignorantly by whether or
not these "old" Standards are "reaffirmed" - but by when and how the NEW
Standard is progressed (and made as clear and correct as possible)

C) That *if* the '85 Standard and the Intrinsic Function Amendment are
withdrawn (by ANSI - not ISO) that it makes NO sense for ANSI to "reaffirm"
the "Correction Amendment" which will end up as "changes" to a
non-recognized document.

  ***

Having said what I would like to see come in as comments, I would still like
to see as many people comment by the end of next Monday EVEN if they all say
that I am "stupid" and a "trouble maker" and that of course ANSI should
reaffirm the existing Standards.
```

#### ↳ Re: STD - (US) Reaffirmation period ends next Monday

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T13:09:32-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bngm2$9rd$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
William M. Klein <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote in message
news:9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net...

<<The US Public Review period ends next Monday, April 23.  If you have any
comments about the "reaffirmation" of the US (not ISO) '85 Standard,
Intrinsic Function Module, or Correction Amendment,  NOW is the time to send
them  ...>>

I would suggest that anyone considering comments opposing reaffirmation
would not be well-equipped to make such a recommendation without having
first read, digested and carefully considered the articles at
http://www.cobolreport.com/columnists/don/, most especially the one at
http://www.cobolreport.com/columnists/don/part2.html.

    -Chuck Stevens
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: STD - (US) Reaffirmation period ends next Monday

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T01:45:39+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae042f4_1@my.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net> <9bngm2$9rd$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`

```
Chuck,

I re-read the articles you referenced. Re-considered my position in the
light of the 4 articles, including your own and Thane's.

I wondered why you didn't reference any articles or comments on  the
opposite side of the fence (there was a good one from me which has since
been removed from the site... and the site no longer solicits Reader
comments... <G> makes me wonder if pressure was brought to bear on the
Editors?)

COBOLReport is a sponsored site and some of what I wrote would not have been
pleasing to the sponsors...or indeed, anyone on the J4 committee (oops, I
forgot, that includes you, doesn't it, Chuck? Didn't see any need to declare
an interest when suggesting people vote in favour of re-affirmation?

Still, I have to admit, If I was connected with a committee that took 10
years to achieve nothing, I probably wouldn't want to tell people about it,
either... )

"anyone considering comments opposing reaffirmation
>would not be well-equipped to make such a recommendation without having
>first read, digested and carefully considered the articles..."

Fair enough.

Did that, here's my comments...by an amazing coincidence, they are the same
comments which were recently expunged from the COBOLReport Web Site...Guess
my mind hasn't changed...

"While it is good that COBOLReport has chosen to balance the arguments from
Bill Klein against re-affirmation, I for one, am concerned that the
counter arguments are all from people who have a vested interest in the
standard and the status quo being maintained.

It is NOT a simple issue of Big Bad Bill de-railing the ANSI standard for
the sake of mischief. The FACT is that there were 10 years to get this
sorted and it didn't happen. It can reasonably be argued that if it can't
be fixed in 10 years, it is unlikely to be fixed in 1 more.

I would like to see the Committee and the four people who jointly wrote to
refute Bill's point, show cause WHY the COBOL community should continue to
trust them with the task of maintaining the COBOL standard when they have
already demonstrated that they are not up to it.

An administrative glitch?!

And their processes and procedures are so unwieldy that nothing can be
done about it?

As for the "bad press", let those who are responsible cop it.

There is nothing wrong with COBOL; there is much wrong with the
bureaucracy that is supposed to administer it.

Bill Klein has provided a timely "wake up" call to the effect that the
standard is badly de-railed. Having worked for many years in committees
dealing with it I am sure that he has not undertaken this action lightly.

COBOL will certainly come through this but many of us will be questioning
whether COBOL and the COBOL community are being adequately served by the
ANSI standard and procedures.

Could it be time for a fresh look?"

Pete.

Chuck Stevens wrote in message <9bngm2$9rd$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>...
>William M. Klein <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote in message
>news:9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net...
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…

send
>them  ...>>
>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```
>I wondered why you didn't reference any articles or comments on  the
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…

Since posting the above I have corresponded with the Editor of COBOLReport
and am relieved and pleased to hear that the Editorial integrity of this
site is not in question.

My response has not been expunged; I was just unable to find it. (Totally my
own fault and no reflection on the site.)

I therefore freely and unreservedly withdraw the suggestion made above, and
sincerely apologise for any doubts that may have been raised by my comment.

COBOLReport continues to provide a valuable service to the COBOL community.

Pete.
```

#### ↳ Re: STD - (US) Reaffirmation period ends next Monday

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T16:37:07-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bnsqj$lte$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`
- **References:** `<9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
William M. Klein <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote in message
news:9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net...

<<A) J4 should spend its time and resources on "finishing up" the revision
of the Standard (and doing work on its planned "technical reports") and
*not* take up (again) the burden of "maintaining" these current COBOL
standards - that are certainly "functionally frozen" and that I think those
vendors that do support - will continue to support whether or not there is
an ANSI Standard.>>

Speaking as someone who is called upon to maintain compilers for a compiler
vendor, I can state from personal experience that customers feel much freer
to demand their "pet projects" from compiler vendors when there is no
standard in force to which that vendor can appeal, and the implementors have
much less leverage in striving to adhere to those standards if they are not
in force.

That has nothing to do with what the terms "in force" (or for that matter
"reaffirmed" or "withdrawn") actually mean in J4/NCITS/ANSI
process-description documents.  It does have a great deal to do with whether
ANSI (or for that matter ISO) standards are *perceived* by the customer as
having direct, *current* relevance to the product they're using.   It doesn'
t really matter to me as an implementor whether they are "functionally
frozen", whether maintenance amendments are being produced, or whether
interpretations are promulgated; what matters is not just that the standard
exists but  more importantly that it continues to be designated as having
official standing in this country.    The fact that it has that standing
today is important to me.

Which leads directly to ...

<<B) The "perception" about COBOL is *not* impacted ignorantly by whether or
not these "old" Standards are "reaffirmed" - but by when and how the NEW
Standard is progressed (and made as clear and correct as possible) >>

A number of the public comments in support of reaffirmation received thus
far have explicitly stated that *failing to reaffirm* those standards would
have a direct and deleterious economic impact on them, on their companies,
and on their customers.   Some claim a *possible*, some a *probable*, and
some a *certain* impact, all deleterious.  These are not comments about the
Final Committee Draft, they are specifically comments *supporting
reaffirmation*.    These submitters assert that the public perception that
COBOL is diminished if ANSI fails to reaffirm the standard, and they support
reaffirmation on economic grounds.

Whether the public perception of COBOL ought reasonably to be diminished by
a failure to reaffirm the standard or not isn't particularly important;
these submitters claim they will suffer if the current standard is not
reaffirmed.  What seems to  matter to them, and to their financial
well-being, is how their customers see it, and they seem to believe their
customers want the standard to continue in force.   The submitters'
ignorance isn't relevant, and neither is the ignorance of the customer that
might not be intimately familiar with the standards process.  These folks
claim that if the standards aren't reaffirmed and are withdrawn, they and
their companies will suffer.  Whether the perception is born out of
ignorance anywhere up or down the financial food chain isn't important to
that financial bottom line.

<< C) That *if* the '85 Standard and the Intrinsic Function Amendment are
withdrawn (by ANSI - not ISO) that it makes NO sense for ANSI to "reaffirm"
the "Correction Amendment" which will end up as "changes" to a
non-recognized document.>>

I'll leave this for others to argue, but I am by no means convinced that "NO
sense" is the whole and complete truth.

    -Chuck Stevens
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: STD - (US) Reaffirmation period ends next Monday

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T02:40:37+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae04d95_1@my.newsfeeds.com>`
- **References:** `<9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net> <9bnsqj$lte$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>`

```
Hmmm....

Comments interspersed below...

Pete.

Chuck Stevens wrote in message <9bnsqj$lte$1@mail.pl.unisys.com>...
>William M. Klein <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote in message
>news:9bmqnb$phk$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net...
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>standard in force to which that vendor can appeal, and the implementors
have
>much less leverage in striving to adhere to those standards if they are not
>in force.
<<<<
Speaking as someone who is called upon to USE compilers from a compiler
vendor, I can state from personal experience that, irrespective of what "pet
Projects" customers request, vendors will implement whatever they want to
(and that includes stuff that is or isn't in the Standard. It's just called
"Vendor specific extensions to the standard" and is totally driven by
Marketing.) If vendors perceive a Market Place for a given extension they
will implement it: If they can't, they won't (and they shouldn't...).

>
>That has nothing to do with what the terms "in force" (or for that matter
>"reaffirmed" or "withdrawn") actually mean in J4/NCITS/ANSI
>process-description documents.  It does have a great deal to do with
whether
>ANSI (or for that matter ISO) standards are *perceived* by the customer as
>having direct, *current* relevance to the product they're using.   It
doesn'
>t really matter to me as an implementor whether they are "functionally
>frozen", whether maintenance amendments are being produced, or whether
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Why? Will your job cease if the standard is withdrawn? (I think and hope
not...)

What you are saying here is that it is necessary to have an "Official"
standard to give COBOL "respectability" and to have a "yardstick" to appeal
to when users want new features.

OK, consider this... There were 10 years in which to produce such a
yardstick. It hasn't happened and, as a result, the standard now lapses.

Why? Because those are the rules.

Why can't we change the rules, if having a standard is so critical?  Because
the organisation which makes the rules has its head so far up its arse that
it can't change ANYTHING... If it was a dynamic, responsive, "can-do" kind
of body, it wouldn't have taken 10 bloody years to NOT produce a
standard...!!!

As for "respectability" before you attain that you need CREDIBILITY.

Do you honestly think that COBOL's credibility as a viable language for
commercial application development has been improved by the current fiasco?
("Hey, should we really entrust our mission critical system development to a
Language which is so complex they can't produce a standard for it in ten
years? Naw...let's go Java.....")

And who is responsible? ANSI and the committees under its umbrella.

COBOL will be a lot better off without them.


>>>
>Which leads directly to ...
>
><<B) The "perception" about COBOL is *not* impacted ignorantly by whether
or
>not these "old" Standards are "reaffirmed" - but by when and how the NEW
>Standard is progressed (and made as clear and correct as possible) >>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>COBOL is diminished if ANSI fails to reaffirm the standard, and they
support
>reaffirmation on economic grounds.
>
>Whether the public perception of COBOL ought reasonably to be diminished by
>a failure to reaffirm the standard or not isn't particularly important;
<<<
Agree with that...!

>>>
>these submitters claim they will suffer if the current standard is not
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>that financial bottom line.
<<<
Good argument. Sadly, there is no connection between the "financial bottom
line" and what the companies believe. (At least, there is none that can be
established from the above well made argument. History has shown that on
many occasions when companies believed something was going to be
"deleterious to the financial bottom line" the facts turned out different.
In some cases the expected disaster was actually beneficial...(Decimal
currency conversions in South Africa, UK, and New Zealand, introduction of
VAT in the UK, GST in Australia, the year 2000, and so on...)

>>>
><< C) That *if* the '85 Standard and the Intrinsic Function Amendment are
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>I'll leave this for others to argue, but I am by no means convinced that
"NO
>sense" is the whole and complete truth.
<<<
Yes, I agree this is academic. Certainly arguable, but not important.

BOTTOM LINE:

Unless the rules are changed, the Standard will NOT be re-affirmed. (It
cannot legally be...)

It doesn't matter whether everybody thinks it should be or not, it WON'T be.

(Personally, I'm not losing any sleep over this because, the way I see it,
either way will be good for COBOL.)

The ANSI organization and the committees delegated by it to handle the COBOL
standard are the ONLY ones culpable. They have shot themselves (and COBOL)
in the foot.

It is time the procedures and processes implemented by ANSI were overhauled
and brought into the 21st century. The Management Style should be reviewed
and a much more open and responsive approach should be encouraged.

I have some very strong ideas about how this could be done but I won't go
into them here.

It is my sincere hope that at least some lessons can be learned from this
and it will never happen again. Until that is guaranteed, COBOL is best
served by complete dissociation from ANSI.

Pete.




-----= Posted via Newsfeeds.Com, Uncensored Usenet News =-----
http://www.newsfeeds.com - The #1 Newsgroup Service in the World!
-----==  Over 80,000 Newsgroups - 16 Different Servers! =-----
```

## Thread: microfocus personal: occurs depending subsidiary to occurs only allowed wih odoslide — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### microfocus personal: occurs depending subsidiary to occurs only allowed wih odoslide

- **From:** goble@gtech (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T14:32:07+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3adef447.29001622@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```
Hi All;

First off this is not homework, just a personal attempt.

Iam tring to use this table...;

01 w1000-score-table.
   05 1100-stage OCCURS 1 TO 4 TIMES
      DEPENDING ON w320-stages INDEXED BY w1100-stage-inx.
     10 1110-series OCCURS 4 TO 18 TIMES
        DEPENDING ON w330-series INDEXED BY w1110-series-inx.
       15 w1115-shot OCCURS 2 TO 10 TIMES
          DEPENDING ON w340-shots INDEXED BY w1115-shot-inx.
         20 w1115a-shot          Pic x(01).

But Microfocus personal Cobol complains with;

occurs depending subsidiary to occurs only allowed wih odoslide 

However it does compile as;

01 w1000-score-table.
   05 1100-stage OCCURS 4 TIMES
	INDEXED BY w1100-stage-inx.
     10 1110-series OCCURS 18 TIMES
        	INDEXED BY w1110-series-inx.
       15 w1115-shot OCCURS 10 TIMES
          		INDEXED BY w1115-shot-inx.
         20 w1115a-shot          Pic x(01).

Is there any other way to do this?

Background.

Iam need to enter scores for different matches. Each match is made up
of x stages, y series, z scores. A score ranges from 0 to 10. To save
space and keystrokes, I will use "X" for 10.

Once the scores are entered by the user I will write them to a file

01 output-file
  05 ....
  05 f350-scores                      Pic x(90). 

some match examples...

match stages series scores
1	4    1,2,4	2,3,5,6
2 	3	4	5
3	2       6       5
4  	2	1,5	10
5	1	6	10
6 	1	4	10
```

#### ↳ Re: microfocus personal: occurs depending subsidiary to occurs only allowed wih odoslide

- **From:** "Alister Munro" <alister@specsoft.freeserve.co.uk>
- **Date:** 2001-04-20T06:10:03+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bocje$k03$1@news8.svr.pol.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<3adef447.29001622@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```
Check out the ODOSLIDE compiler directive which can
be used within the $SET command.

$SET ODOSLIDE should be the first line within your
program (the $ symbol starts in column 7).

"David. E. Goble" <goble@gtech> wrote in message
news:3adef447.29001622@news.adelaide.on.net...
> Hi All;
>
…[51 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

<a id="t-c5042c18df"></a>
## Thread: Help: I have gotten myself into logic knotes of confussion wih my program — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help: I have gotten myself into logic knotes of confussion wih my program

- **From:** goble@gtech (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T14:32:08+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3adef44f.29010035@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```
Hi All;

Could anyone help me out. I have gotten tied up in knotes tring to
write this program. See MicroFocus Personal Cobol code and the
matches.mst below.

What I have compiles. What I want is to get the members to loop thru
the same as the matches, when I select all members and to enter
scores.

Any help is welcomed on this and/or anything else you may find?
Thanks.

********* Matches.mst  *********
Free Pistol                  20610600555540510465
Rapid Fire                   21205600582565535480
Standard Pistol              21205600570555530500
Centre Fire                  21205600583565540500
Sport Pistol                 21205600580555520480
Junior Sport Pistol          21205600580550510465
Air Pistol - 60 Shot         10610600575560540500
Air Pistol - Ladies          10410600380365350320
Air Pistol - Junior (F)      10410600373360340300
Air Pistol - Junior (M)      10610600565540510450
Service Pistol               41806900870850800750
Service Pistol - Unrestricted41806900885870840800
Black Powder - 50 metre      20805600373350320290
Black Powder - Aggregate     20805600380360340300
Action Pistol                00000100099096090080

********* Program code *********
       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
      *************************
       PROGRAM-ID. JOB1.
       AUTHOR. David. E. Goble.
       INSTALLATION. Gtech_Computing
                     for
                     Kangaroo Island Pistol Club.
       DATE-WRITTEN. 26/11/1999.
       DATE-COMPILED.
       SECURITY. Gtech_Computing, free to distribute.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
      **********************
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.
      *----------------------
       SOURCE-COMPUTER. 486DX2-66.
       OBJECT-COMPUTER. 386SX-33.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
      *---------------------
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT f100-matches-file
               ASSIGN TO DISK "constant/Matches.mst"
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
               ACCESS IS SEQUENTIAL
               FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.
           SELECT f200-members-file
               ASSIGN TO DISK "data/members.dat"
               ORGANIZATION IS RELATIVE
               ACCESS IS DYNAMIC RELATIVE KEY IS w150-Rel-Key
               FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.
           SELECT f300-scores-file
               ASSIGN TO DISK "data/scores.dat"
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
               ACCESS IS SEQUENTIAL
               FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.
       DATA DIVISION.
      ***************
       FILE SECTION.
      *-------------
       FD  f100-matches-file
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
           RECORD CONTAINS 49 CHARACTERS
           DATA RECORD IS f100-matches-rec.
       01 f100-matches-rec.
           05 f110-matches-match               Pic x(29).
           05 f115-stages                      Pic 9(01).
           05 f116-series                      Pic 9(02).
           05 f117-shots                       Pic 9(02).
           05 f118-matches-max                 Pic 9(03).
           05 f120-grades-gradem               Pic 9(03).
           05 f130-grades-gradea               Pic 9(03).
           05 f140-grades-gradeb               Pic 9(03).
           05 f150-grades-gradec               Pic 9(03).
       FD  f200-members-file
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
           RECORD CONTAINS 33 CHARACTERS
           DATA RECORD IS f200-members-rec.
       01 f200-members-rec                     Pic x(30).
       FD  f300-scores-file
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
           RECORD CONTAINS 136 CHARACTERS
           DATA RECORD IS f300-scores-rec.
       01 f300-scores-rec.
           05 f310-shooters-name.
               10 f311-sname-first             Pic x(15).
               10 f312-sname-last              Pic x(15).
           05 f320-match-shot                  Pic 9(02).
           05 f330-match-date.
               10  f331-year                   Pic 9(04).
               10  f332-month                  Pic 9(02).
               10  f333-day                    Pic 9(02).
           05 f340-calibra                     Pic 9(03).
           05 f350-aggregate                   Pic 9(03).
           05 f350-scores                      Pic x(90).
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
      *------------------------
       01 w100-program-switches.
           05 w110-end-of-file                 Pic x.
               88 w111-eof                     value 'y'.
           05 w120-file-status                 Pic x(02).
           05 w130-Menu-selection              Pic 9.
           05 w140-press-a-key                 Pic x.
           05 w150-Rel-Key                     Pic 9(03).
       01 w200-date-variables.
           05  w210-system-date.
               10  w211-year                   Pic 9(04).
               10  w212-month                  Pic 9(02).
               10  w213-day                    Pic 9(02).
           05 w220-Date                        Pic x(10).
       01 w300-vars.
           05 w310-lengths.
               10 w311-lenA                    Pic 9(02).
               10 w312-lenB                    Pic 9(02).
               10 w313-center                  Pic 9(02).
           05 w320-stages                      Pic 9(01).
           05 w330-series                      Pic 9(02).
           05 w340-shots                       Pic 9(02).

       01 w400-members-rec.
           05 w410-first-key                   Pic 9(03).
           05 w420-lst-key                     Pic 9(03).
           05 filler                           Pic x(27).
       01 w500-members-rec.
           05 w510-members-name.
               10 w511-mname-first             Pic x(15).
               10 w512-mname-last              Pic x(15).
       01 w600-actions.
           05 a-to-continue                    Pic x(32)
                               value "Press the return key to
continue".
           05 a-anymore                        Pic x(26)
                                     value "Any more members?
[Yy/Nn]:".
           05 match-selection                  Pic 9(02).
           05 member-selection                 Pic 9(03).
           05 selector                         Pic x.
           05 add-new-member                   Pic x(30)
                                                value "Add New
Members".
           05 all-members                      Pic x(30)
                                                    value "All
Members".
           05 instructions.
               10 i1                           Pic x(80)
                       value "To move up press [U/u/,/<], To move
down".
               10 i2                           Pic x(80)
           value "press [D/d/./>], To select current value press
[S/s]".
               10 i3                           Pic x(80)
                                     value "press [Q/q, D/d, R/r or
E]".
       01 w700-matches-table.
           05 w705-match-sub                   Pic 9(02).
           05 w710-by-match OCCURS 16 INDEXED BY w710-match-inx.
               10 w711-match                   Pic x(33).
               10 w711a-stages                 Pic 9(01).
               10 w711b-series                 Pic 9(02).
               10 w711c-shots                  Pic 9(02).
               10 w711d-out-of                 Pic 9(03).
               10 w712-by-grade OCCURS 4 INDEXED BY w912-grade-inx.
                   15 w712b-grade              Pic 9(03).
       01 w800-line.
           05 filler                           Pic x(40)
                       value
"----------------------------------------".
           05 filler                           Pic x(40)
                       value
"----------------------------------------".
       01 w900-msg.
           05 w990-display-message             Pic x(70).
           05 w980-display-action              Pic x(80).
       01 w-score-sheet.
           05 1100-stage OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY w1100-stage-inx.
               10 1110-series OCCURS 18 TIMES
                                            INDEXED BY
w1110-series-inx.
                   15 w1115-shot OCCURS 10 TIMES
                                              INDEXED BY
w1115-shot-inx.
                       20 w1115a-shot          Pic x.
       SCREEN SECTION.
      *---------------
       01 ss100-Main-screen.
           05 BLANK SCREEN.
           05 LINE  1 COLUMN 01 value "Kangaroo Island Pistol Club".
           05         COLUMN 64 value "DATE: ".
           05         COLUMN 70 Pic x(10) from w220-Date.
       01 ss200-Member-Selection.
           05 LINE  3 COLUMN 16
                            value
"-----------------------------------".
           05 LINE  4 COLUMN 02 value "Members Name: |".
           05         COLUMN 18 Pic x(15) FROM w511-mname-first.
           05         COLUMN 34 PIC x(15) FROM w512-mname-last.
           05         COLUMN 50 value "|".
           05 LINE  5 COLUMN 16
                            value
"-----------------------------------".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss210-member-detail.
           05 LINE 3 COLUMN 01 value "Members first name: ".
           05        COLUMN 21 Pic x(15) TO w511-mname-first.
           05 LINE 5 COLUMN 10 value "last name: ".
           05        COLUMN 21 Pic x(15) TO w512-mname-last.

       01 ss300-Match-Selection.
           05 LINE  7 COLUMN 16
                              value
"---------------------------------".
           05 LINE  8 COLUMN 04 value "Match Type: |".
           05         COLUMN 18 Pic x(33) FROM

w711-match(match-selection).
           05         COLUMN 48 value "|".
           05 LINE  9 COLUMN 16
                              value
"---------------------------------".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss400-Button-Selection.
           05 LINE 12 COLUMN 08 value "Score".
           05 LINE 13 COLUMN 02 value "Q uick".
           05         COLUMN 10 value "D etailed".
           05         COLUMN 25 value "R eport".
           05         COLUMN 43 value "E nd program".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss500-score-sheet.
           05 LINE  3 COLUMN 23 Pic x(33) FROM

w711-match(match-selection).
           05 LINE  5 COLUMN 01 value "Name:".
           05         COLUMN 07 Pic x(15) FROM w511-mname-first.
           05         COLUMN 21 PIC x(15) FROM w512-mname-last.
           05 LINE  7 COLUMN 01 value "Date".
           05         COLUMN 20 value "No. of Shots".
           05         COLUMN 35 value "Cal:".
           05         COLUMN 40 Pic 9(03) TO f340-calibra.
           05 LINE  8 COLUMN 02 Pic 9(02) TO f333-day.
           05         COLUMN 04 value "/".
           05         COLUMN 05 Pic 9(02) TO f332-month.
           05         COLUMN 07 value "/".
           05         COLUMN 08 Pic 9(04) TO f331-year.
           05         COLUMN 12 value " M   1   2   3   4   5   6 ".
           05         COLUMN 40 value "Score".
           05 LINE  9 COLUMN 01 value " ".
       01 ss600-pigeon-holes.
           05         COLUMN 12 Pic 9(01) FROM w330-series.
           05         COLUMN 14 value " |   |   |   |   |   |   |".
       01 ss900-msg-screen.
           05 LINE 22 COLUMN 01 value "MESSAGE: ".
           05 LINE 23 COLUMN 10 Pic x(70) FROM w990-display-message.
           05 LINE 24 COLUMN 01 Pic x(80) FROM w980-display-action.
           05 LINE 24 COLUMN 65 Pic x TO w140-press-a-key.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      ********************
       0000000-MAIN-MODULE.
      *====================
           PERFORM 1000000-INITIALISE-MODULE.
           PERFORM 2000000-PROCESS-MODULE
               UNTIL w111-eof.
           PERFORM 3000000-END-MODULE.
           STOP RUN.
       1000000-INITIALISE-MODULE.
      *==========================
           PERFORM 1300000-SET-DATE.
           PERFORM 1400000-INITIALISE-VARS.
       1300000-SET-DATE.
      *=================
           MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO w210-system-date.
           STRING  w213-day "/"
                   w212-month "/"
                   w211-year
                   DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   INTO w220-Date
           END-STRING.
       1400000-INITIALISE-VARS.
      *========================
          MOVE ZEROS TO w130-Menu-selection.
          MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                         w980-display-action.
          MOVE SPACES TO  w140-press-a-key.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
          MOVE 16 TO match-selection.
          MOVE ZEROS TO member-selection.
          MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name.
          OPEN INPUT f100-matches-file
               I-O f200-members-file.
          PERFORM 141000-LOAD-ARRAY
               VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
               UNTIL w710-match-inx>15 OR w111-eof.
          PERFORM 142000-ADD-TO-ARRAY.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
          PERFORM 143000-READ-MEMBER-HEADER.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
       141000-LOAD-ARRAY.
      *==================
           READ f100-matches-file
               AT END
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
               NOT AT END
                   PERFORM 141100-LOAD-MATCH
           END-READ.
       141100-LOAD-MATCH.
      *==================
           MOVE f110-matches-match TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f115-stages TO w711a-stages(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f116-series TO w711b-series(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f117-shots TO w711c-shots(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f118-matches-max TO w711d-out-of(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f120-grades-gradem TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 1).
           MOVE f130-grades-gradea TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 2).
           MOVE f140-grades-gradeb TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 3).
           MOVE f150-grades-gradec TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 4).
       142000-ADD-TO-ARRAY.
      *====================
           MOVE "All Matches" TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE ZEROS TO w711a-stages(w710-match-inx)
                         w711b-series(w710-match-inx)
                         w711c-shots(w710-match-inx)
                         w711d-out-of(w710-match-inx)
                         w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 1)
                         w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 2)
                         w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 3)
                         w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 4).
       143000-READ-MEMBER-HEADER.
      *==========================
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           MOVE 1 TO w150-Rel-Key.
           READ f200-members-file INTO w400-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "There are no members, create members [Yy/Nn]"
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG
                   IF(NOT w111-eof)
                   THEN
      * get user to enter member details... etc...
      * then update/create header file...
                       MOVE 3 TO w410-first-key
                                 w420-lst-key
                       PERFORM 143100-GET-MEMBER-DETAILS UNTIL
w111-eof
                   ELSE
                       MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
                   END-IF
               NOT INVALID KEY
                   MOVE w420-lst-key TO w150-Rel-key
           END-READ.
       143100-GET-MEMBER-DETAILS.
      *==========================
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           DISPLAY ss210-member-detail.
           ACCEPT ss210-member-detail.
           MOVE a-anymore TO w980-display-action.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-Display-message.
           PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
           MOVE w140-press-a-key TO w110-end-of-file.
           IF(NOT w111-eof)
           THEN
               IF(w150-Rel-key >= w420-lst-key)
               THEN
                   ADD 1 TO w420-lst-key
               END-IF
               PERFORM 143110-WRITE-MEMBER-HEADER
               MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-IF.
           PERFORM 143120-WRITE-MEMBER-REC.
       143110-WRITE-MEMBER-HEADER.
      *===========================
           MOVE 1 to w150-Rel-Key
           WRITE f200-members-rec FROM w400-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   IF(w120-file-status=22)
                   THEN
                       PERFORM 143111-REWRITE-MEMBER-HEADER
                   ELSE
                       STRING "error writing member header"
                              w120-file-status
                           DELIMITED BY SIZE
                               INTO w990-display-message
                       END-STRING
                       PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                   END-IF
           END-WRITE.
       143111-REWRITE-MEMBER-HEADER.
      *=============================
           REWRITE f200-members-rec FROM w400-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   STRING "error writing member header"
                           w120-file-status
                       DELIMITED BY SIZE
                           INTO w990-display-message
                   END-STRING
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
           END-REWRITE.
       143120-WRITE-MEMBER-REC.
      *========================
           MOVE w420-lst-key TO w150-Rel-Key
                                member-selection.
           WRITE f200-members-rec FROM w500-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   STRING "error writing member details"
                          w120-file-status
                       DELIMITED BY SIZE
                          INTO w990-display-message
                   END-STRING
               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
           END-WRITE.
       2000000-PROCESS-MODULE.
      *=======================
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           PERFORM 2100000-MEMBERS UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
           PERFORM 2200000-MATCH-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
      * buttons... quick score/detailed score/report/quit-exit/cancel
           DISPLAY ss400-Button-Selection.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-Display-message.
           MOVE i3 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss400-Button-Selection.
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           PERFORM 2300000-BUTTON-SELECTION.
       2100000-MEMBERS.
      *================
           PERFORM 2110000-MEMBER-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof.
           IF(member-selection EQUALS ZEROS)
           THEN
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               PERFORM 143100-GET-MEMBER-DETAILS UNTIL w111-eof
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen
           END-IF.
           PERFORM 2110000-MEMBER-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof.
       2110000-MEMBER-SELECTION.
      *========================
           DISPLAY ss200-Member-Selection.
           MOVE i1 TO w990-display-message.
           MOVE i2 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss200-Member-Selection.
           EVALUATE selector
               WHEN "U"
               WHEN "u"
               WHEN "<"
               WHEN ","
                   PERFORM 2111000-MOVE-MEMBER-UP
               WHEN "D"
               WHEN "d"
               WHEN ">"
               WHEN "."
                   PERFORM 2112000-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN
               WHEN "S"
               WHEN "s"
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO selector.
           PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC.
       2111000-MOVE-MEMBER-UP.
      *=======================
           IF(member-selection>=w420-lst-key)
           THEN
               MOVE ZEROS TO member-selection
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO member-selection
           END-IF.
           EVALUATE member-selection
               WHEN ZERO
                   MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 1
                   ADD 1 TO member-selection
                   MOVE all-members TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 2
                   MOVE all-members TO w510-members-name
           END-EVALUATE.
       2112000-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN.
      *=========================
           IF(member-selection<=ZEROS)
           THEN
               MOVE w420-lst-key TO member-selection
           ELSE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM member-selection
           END-IF.
           EVALUATE member-selection
               WHEN ZERO
                   MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 1
                   SUBTRACT 1 FROM member-selection
                   MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 2
                   MOVE all-members TO w510-members-name
           END-EVALUATE.
       2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC.
      *========================
           IF(member-selection>=w410-first-key)
           THEN
               MOVE member-selection TO w150-Rel-Key
               READ f200-members-file INTO w500-members-rec
                   INVALID KEY
                       DISPLAY "error in rel key of members file"
               END-READ
           END-IF.
       2200000-MATCH-SELECTION.
      *========================
           DISPLAY ss300-Match-Selection.
           MOVE i1 TO w990-display-message.
           MOVE i2 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss300-Match-Selection.
           EVALUATE selector
               WHEN "U"
               WHEN "u"
               WHEN "<"
               WHEN ","
                   PERFORM 2210000-MOVE-MATCH-UP
               WHEN "D"
               WHEN "d"
               WHEN ">"
               WHEN "."
                   PERFORM 2220000-MOVE-MATCH-DOWN
               WHEN "S"
               WHEN "s"
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO selector.
       2210000-MOVE-MATCH-UP.
      *======================
           IF(match-selection>=16)
           THEN
               MOVE 1 TO match-selection
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO match-selection
           END-IF.
       2220000-MOVE-MATCH-DOWN.
      *========================
           IF(match-selection<=1)
           THEN
               MOVE 16 TO match-selection
           ELSE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM match-selection
           END-IF.
       2300000-BUTTON-SELECTION.
      *=========================
           EVALUATE selector
               WHEN "Q"
               WHEN "q"
                   PERFORM 2310000-QUICK-SCORE
               WHEN "D"
               WHEN "d"
                   PERFORM 2320000-DETAILED-SCORE
               WHEN "D"
               WHEN "d"
                   PERFORM 2330000-REPORT
               WHEN "E"
               WHEN "e"
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO selector.
       2310000-QUICK-SCORE.
      *====================
           PERFORM 2311000-SINGLE-OR-MULTI-MATCH.
       2311000-SINGLE-OR-MULTI-MATCH.
      *==============================
           IF(match-selection EQUALS 16)
           THEN
               PERFORM 2311100-LOOP-THRU-MATCHES
                   VARYING match-selection FROM 1 BY 1
                       UNTIL match-selection EQUALS 16 OR w111-eof
           ELSE
               PERFORM 2311100-LOOP-THRU-MATCHES
           END-IF.
       2311100-LOOP-THRU-MATCHES.
      *==========================
           IF(member-selection>=w410-first-key)
           THEN
               PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO member-selection
               PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC
           END-IF.

           DISPLAY ss500-score-sheet.
      *    ACCEPT ss500-score-sheet.
       2320000-DETAILED-SCORE.
      *=======================
           PERFORM 2311000-SINGLE-OR-MULTI-MATCH.
      * need to know member-selection [all/individual]
      * start with match...
      * read match/es sequentially until eof/table
      *    display match name
      *    need to ask user for date of match
      * next read thru member/s until eof
      *    ask user for members calibra
      *    for each stage
      *        for each series
      *            ask user for each shot
           DISPLAY ss500-score-sheet.
       2330000-REPORT.
      *===============
       2900000-ERROR.
      *==============
           MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(a-to-continue) TO w311-lenA.
           MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(w980-display-action) TO w312-lenB.
           COMPUTE w313-center=(w312-lenB - w311-lenA)/2.
           MOVE a-to-continue TO w980-display-action(w313-center:).
           PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
       2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
      *=====================
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss900-msg-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
       3000000-END-MODULE.
      *===================
```

```
wrote:

Hi Howard;

Ok, after selecting a match ie either an idividual match or the option
"all matchs" and after selecting a member or the option "all members".

The user needs to select to enter quick scores, detailed scores,
select reports or end program.

When the user selects to enter scores either quick scores or detailed
scores. The user sees this screen;

Kangaroo Island Pistol Club                           DATE: 20/04/2001


                      Free Pistol



Name: David         Goble



Date               No. of Shots   Cal: 000

 00/00/0000 M   1   2   3   4   5   6  Score



Which allows the user to either enter detail scores or a quick score.

If the selected match was "all matches" the match header will and does
loop thru all matches. I want the Name header to do the samething with
the members. That is if the option "all members" was selected then it
should loop thru the members names. Note that for each match the name
header should loop thru all members, then loop to the next match.

It is the members part I am having trouble figuring out how to do...?
```

```
wouldn't be the best person to answer your question as I don't use

your environment includes a debugger though which will allow you to step
thru your code interactively.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help: I have gotten myself into logic knotes of confussion wih my program

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-19T18:35:27+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ADF2FE8.BC65D181@home.com>`
- **References:** `<3adef44f.29010035@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```


"David. E. Goble" wrote:

> Hi All;
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>

David,

"I have gotten tied up in knots......". Not really surprised <G>. I've put
it through Net Express, but without additional data files - I'm not going
to test.

However - having got a very clearly laid out program I think you have
given yourself a 'future' problem with your very precise naming of things
:-

(1) - f100-matches-file
       01 f100-matches-rec.
           05 f110-matches-match               Pic x(29).
           05 f115-stages                      Pic 9(01).
           05 f116-series                      Pic 9(02).
           05 f117-shots                       Pic 9(02).
           05 f118-matches-max                 Pic 9(03).
           05 f120-grades-gradem               Pic 9(03).
           05 f130-grades-gradea               Pic 9(03).
           05 f140-grades-gradeb               Pic 9(03).
           05 f150-grades-gradec               Pic 9(03).

try : FD Matches-File. (don't need record size etc.- you really only need
this stuff if you are going to have variable length records - see why
people complain COBOL is verbose <G>)

FD Matches-file.

       01 matches-rec.
           05 matches-match               Pic x(29).
           05 matches-stages                      Pic 9(01).
           05 matches-series                      Pic 9(02).
           05 matches-shots                       Pic 9(02).
            05 matches-etc....

Much easier to remember, and will save you a lot of cross-referencing back
for  f????-reference. OK so this is a one shot program, BUT - expand the
system and you will have an advantage by using copy files for both
FILE-CONTROL and FILE SECTION, because some of these files could pop up
elsewhere in other programs..

(2) - Similarly simplify the naming of your screen items, with something
you can remember when coding.

.       01 S1-Main-screen.
           05 BLANK SCREEN.
           05 LINE  1 COLUMN 01 value "Kangaroo Island Pistol Club".
           05         COLUMN 64 value "DATE: ".
           05         COLUMN 70 Pic x(10) from w220-Date.

    *> Example : above could be more meaningful if instead of w220-Date it
was
    *> called ws-Date-10 or Date10

       01 S2-Member-Selection.
           05 LINE  3 COLUMN 16
                            value

(3) Character testing of 'Selector' - you could short-cut your EVALUATES

05 selector                             pic x.
    88 MoveMemberUp        value "U", "u", "<",  ",".
    88 MoveMemberDown    value  "D", "d", ".", ".".
    88 MoveEnd                    value "S", "s".

Evaluate true
  when MoveMemberUp            PERFORM 2111000-MOVE-MEMBER-UP
  when MoveMemberDown        PERFORM 112000-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN
  when MoveEnd
  when other                                MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
End-evaluate

Note: when 'other' is a 'catch-all for 88 MoveEnd or anything else. You
could differentiate between MoveEnd and 'other' by displaying an "Invalid
character key' message for when 'other'.

Instead of that MOVE 'y' - try (a) initialize - set NotEndOfFile to true
and then
(b) when other set EndOfFile to true

(4) - Paragraph numbering - nothing wrong with :

2112000-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN but a bit easier if you shorten it :

    A20-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN or
    211-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN

(5) ODOSLIDE -

> But Microfocus personal Cobol complains with;
>occurs depending subsidiary to occurs only allowed wih odoslide
>However it does compile as;

Golden rule - if compiler 'complains' - correct it. It may 'appear' to
work, but there's a glitch in there somewhere. Golden Rule #2 - vendor has
set default directives - don't mess around with them unless you absolutely
MUST. So avoid Odoslide and look at problem.

I compiled clean in Net Express - BUT - using your code below - it doesn't
contain a reference to w1000-score-table.

Hope the above gives you some pointers.

Jimmy, Calgary AB

>
> ********* Matches.mst  *********
…[610 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

- **From:** goble@gtech (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T13:37:27+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae03126.1066079@news.adelaide.on.net>`
- **References:** `<3adef44f.29010035@news.adelaide.on.net> <3ADF2FE8.BC65D181@home.com>`

```
On Thu, 19 Apr 2001 18:35:27 GMT, "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
wrote:
>
>"I have gotten tied up in knots......". Not really surprised <G>. I've put
>it through Net Express, but without additional data files - I'm not going
>to test.
>
Hi James;

Thanks for your reply. The only data file needed was the matches.mst
file. The program has been designed to create the member.dat file if
not found. The only other thing is that the files are expected in
folders/directories - ../constant/matches.mst and ../data/member.dat.

About the precise naming conventions, that was the way I was taught to
write Cobol programs in T.A.F.E and at Uni. Its hard to change from
one's initial training :> At least I should use 88 levels more often
:>

Again thanks for your reply.
```

- **From:** floydj@netins.net (Floyd Johnson)

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9bnln0$jp2$1@ins21.netins.net>`

```
I would like to use ODBC with MS Excel.  I understand how to create the
ODBC data source using ODBC, but I am not clear about how to access the
data.  Has anybody tried this using Fujitsu COBOL?  I would like to
present it as a project in my classes next semester.  I could use MS
Sequel Server or Access, but these are presented later as part of our 
Database course.  

The concept seems possible - now if I can figure it out.  I will
experiment during the summer - but any hints or roadblocks would be
appreciated.

Thanks,

Floyd Johnson
```

```
You can try using Q + E database editor.  It allows a macro to make an
ODC connection to run an SQL statement.  This may be very slow, however
but it does work.  Advantage software makes it.
```

## Thread: Comments on draft COBOL 2002 Standard — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### Comments on draft COBOL 2002 Standard

- **From:** Tim Josling <tej@melbpc.org.au>
- **Date:** 2001-04-20T19:53:20+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE00710.31BE32FB@melbpc.org.au>`

```
Here is a copy of my comments on the draft standard.

My main gripes are

- too many features of dubious value are mandatory (screen,
report writer)
- OO should be put in a new language "Object COBOL" rather than
being mandatory in COBOL2002
- too many ridiculous restrictions eg cannot redefine a pointer.
- too many magic numbers eg line length must be less than 255,
literals must be less than 160 bytes, PIC must be less than 50
etc etc etc
- editorially, there is too much use of undefined terms - the
list of definitions and index need major work
- the tightening of copyright

If the tables below are not lined up, change your browser to a
fixed width font. I will upload html to my web page fairly soon
and post a link.

I am not late submitting this becasue different deadlines apply
outside the USA. However I realise that not many of these
comments will be accepted, apart from the typos.

Tim Josling

                   Date        Document

                   19   April  Comment  on   COBOL 2002

Introduction:

My Name is  Tim Josling. I am developing a free COBOL  compiler
as part of a
world wide team. My  comments are written from that perspective.
I am basing
my  comments  on  the  assumption that  the  standards  body 
would wish  to
encourage implementations of the standard.

My email address is tej at melbpc dot org dot au.
```

<a id="t-ef49d003c1"></a>
## Thread: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

- **From:** Tim Josling <tej@melbpc.org.au>
- **Date:** 2001-04-20T20:11:01+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au>`

```
Here is a link to my comments on the draft standard.

http://cobolforgcc.sourceforge.net/cobol2002comments.html

My main gripes are

- too many features of dubious value are mandatory (screen,
report writer)
- OO should be put in a new language "Object COBOL" rather than
being mandatory in COBOL2002
- too many ridiculous restrictions eg cannot redefine a pointer.
- too many magic numbers eg line length must be less than 255,
literals must be less than 160 bytes, PIC must be less than 50
etc etc etc
- editorially, there is too much use of undefined terms - the
list of definitions and index need major work
- the tightening of copyright

If the tables below are not lined up, change your browser to a
fixed width font. I will upload html to my web page fairly soon
and post a link.

I am not late submitting this becasue different deadlines apply
outside the USA. However I realise that not many of these
comments will be accepted, apart from the typos.

Tim Josling
```

#### ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@neo.rr.com.nospam>
- **Date:** 2001-04-20T12:45:11-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<FCF02E2E0F1E7D92.0A8377330D920278.F3D92EC8A966616F@lp.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au>`

```
On Fri, 20 Apr 2001 20:11:01 +1000, Tim Josling <tej@melbpc.org.au>
enlightened us:

Just a few comments interspersed below.


>Here is a link to my comments on the draft standard.
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>report writer)

Dubious value?  Report writer, at least, has a lot of value for shops
that have programmers that know how to use it.  Using Report Writer
can save hours of coding simple reports.  And you don't have to buy
any third party products (DYL-280, CA-EARL e.g.) to do the same thing.


>- OO should be put in a new language "Object COBOL" rather than
>being mandatory in COBOL2002

Hadn't thought of that and don't know enough about OO to say for sure.

>- too many ridiculous restrictions eg cannot redefine a pointer.
>- too many magic numbers eg line length must be less than 255,
>literals must be less than 160 bytes, PIC must be less than 50
>etc etc etc

There has to be limits on these things.  There is no such thing as
infinite storage in any computer platform.

>- editorially, there is too much use of undefined terms - the
>list of definitions and index need major work
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>Tim Josling

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

I'd give my right arm to be ambidextrous!


DON'T DRILL in the Artic National Wildlife Refuge
Read the facts and sign a petition at:

http://www.savepolarbears.org/

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

- **From:** Tim Josling <tej@melbpc.org.au>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T07:09:16+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE0A57C.BB3E556B@melbpc.org.au>`
- **References:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au> <FCF02E2E0F1E7D92.0A8377330D920278.F3D92EC8A966616F@lp.airnews.net>`

```
See below.

SkippyPB wrote:
> >- too many features of dubious value are mandatory (screen,
> >report writer)
> 
> Dubious value?  Report writer, at least, has a lot of value for shops
> that have programmers that know how to use it.

Many shops actually ban the use of report writer. IBM has not
even implemented it in their mainstream compiler (though they
offer a preprocessor that you can use). Some colleagues did some
tests and found it took longer and took more lines of code to
write various reports with report writer than without it. The
Tiny COBOL project elected not to implement it, and instead
provide a facility that allows you to define fonts etc. I think
it is fair to say that the benefits of report writer are dubious
(=able to be doubted)

I don't have a problem with people using report writer, or with
people implementing it. But I disagree that it should be a
mandatory core part of the language.

> 
> >- too many ridiculous restrictions eg cannot redefine a pointer.
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

Agree. I suggested to the committee that they adopt the following
principles

- implementors are encouraged to make 'available memory' the
limit where possible
- they must document any limits
- where they have fixed limits they must meet minimum standards
- the minimum standards should not be a large set of random
numbers (like 31, 50, 150, 255) but should be a small set of easy
to remember numbers eg 10, 100.

> >If the tables below are not lined up, change your browser to a
> >fixed width font. I will upload html to my web page fairly soon
> >and post a link.
> >
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T12:44:01+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE17281.C8A7202C@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au> <FCF02E2E0F1E7D92.0A8377330D920278.F3D92EC8A966616F@lp.airnews.net> <3AE0A57C.BB3E556B@melbpc.org.au>`

```
Tim Josling wrote:

> - the minimum standards should not be a large set of random
> numbers (like 31, 50, 150, 255) but should be a small set of easy
> to remember numbers eg 10, 100.

In what way are 10 and 100 'non-ramdom' and 'easy to remember' that 31
and 255 are not ?

If there is to be a limit to, say, the length of a word, then 10 or 100
are inappropriate (too short or too long) random numbers while 31 is a
reasonable size that packs nicely into some compiler structure.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Tim Josling <tej@melbpc.org.au>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T15:13:37+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE11701.21657F57@melbpc.org.au>`
- **References:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au> <FCF02E2E0F1E7D92.0A8377330D920278.F3D92EC8A966616F@lp.airnews.net> <3AE0A57C.BB3E556B@melbpc.org.au> <3AE17281.C8A7202C@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
Well, for three reasons

1. I am suggesting reducing the number of magic numbers from many
to a smaller number.
2. I am suggesting that vendors be encouraged not to have any
arbitrary limits, which would mean that usually there is nothing
to remember. 
3. Round numbers have fewer bits of entropy and therefore have
less to remember.

For example how many people coudl tell you off the top of their
head what is the maximum length of a name in COBOL? In the new
draft standard? What is the maximum length of a PICTURE string? 

Tim Josling


Richard Plinston wrote:
> 
> Tim Josling wrote:
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> reasonable size that packs nicely into some compiler structure.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-23T07:44:19-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE431B3.B1EDB997@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au> <FCF02E2E0F1E7D92.0A8377330D920278.F3D92EC8A966616F@lp.airnews.net> <3AE0A57C.BB3E556B@melbpc.org.au> <3AE17281.C8A7202C@Azonic.co.nz> <3AE11701.21657F57@melbpc.org.au>`

```
Tim Josling wrote:

> For example how many people coudl tell you off the top of their
> head what is the maximum length of a name in COBOL? In the new
> draft standard? What is the maximum length of a PICTURE string?

I don't know, but I bet all programmers could tell yu off the bottom of their
compile listings when they exceed it.

What the standards give us here is the ability to use the same name with two
different compilers.  It's not important that we remember the size except
when we are creating copy members which might have different sized prefixes
added later, as we will learn quite quickly when we exceed the limits.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

- **From:** David Essex <dessex@arvotek.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-21T07:57:13-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE17599.A4052421@arvotek.net>`
- **References:** `<3AE00B35.86E6A68F@melbpc.org.au> <FCF02E2E0F1E7D92.0A8377330D920278.F3D92EC8A966616F@lp.airnews.net> <3AE0A57C.BB3E556B@melbpc.org.au>`

```
Tim Josling wrote:
> ... 
> SkippyPB wrote:
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> (=able to be doubted)

A Report writer (REPORT SECTION) for TinyCOBOL is currently being
developed by Rildo Pragana.

Information on what is currently under development can be found on the
TC status web page (1) or by browsing at the current TinyCOBOL grammar
on CVS.


1) TinyCOBOL status web page
http://tiny-cobol.sourceforge.net/status.html
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard

- **From:** chims1@aol.com (ChimS1)
- **Date:** 2001-04-22T15:32:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010422113243.07560.00000309@ng-xa1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3AE0A57C.BB3E556B@melbpc.org.au>`

```
The old report writer was hard to use because it didn't give any good visible
clues as to why programs abended while execution occurred during the report
writing routines.

The preprocessor is functional for the current version of Cobol, but it's not
maintainable. It is not readable and it goes against the concept of structured
programming where maintainability and modification should be accepted practices
in good program development. In fact, it seems to be much easier and more
productive to rewrite the whole program and coding your own reporting routines.

Someone mentioned DYL-280 about its facility in report writing. If Cobol can
imitate the power and ease of DYL-280, that would be an added engine for Cobol
programmers without having to learn new languages. I know that NATURAL has
incorporated these facilities for report writer.

As for screen development, the current interface to CICS should be sufficient.
The flow between these two domains need somewhat to be improved. From my point
of view, the problem around here is more cultural than technical. I say this
because I've seen NATURAL can do either way, screen development as good as CICS
or reporting writing as good as DYL-280, without making a fuss.

But then NATURAL is proprietary while Cobol is a common language that is being
attempted for compromise by different vendors according to specific standards
of agreement.

Chim

>Subject: Re: Comments on COBOL 2002 draft Standard
>From: Tim Josling tej@melbpc.org.au 
…[56 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```
