## Thread: Announcement: 18th Testing Computer Software (TCS2001) Conference — 1 msgs

### Announcement: 18th Testing Computer Software (TCS2001) Conference

- **From:** pazpax@earthlink.net (TCS2001)
- **Date:** 2001-04-25T13:38:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.forth
- **Message-ID:** `<pazpax-2504010839030001@1cust216.tnt17.tco2.da.uu.net>`

```

The continuing annual Conference on Testing Computer Software again
assembled a formidable group of experts on every phase of testing. The
latest developments and discoveries of software testing technologies will
be presented and explored, to meet the software development challenges of
the coming years.

             18th International Conference and Exposition on 

                        TESTING COMPUTER SOFTWARE
              Theme: Meeting the New Challenges of Testing
                            June 18-22, 2001
            Washington, D.C. * Hyatt Regency (Bethesda) * USA 
        (Conference: June 20-22; Workshops/Tutorials: June 18-19) 

In cooperation with:
Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) SIGSoft
American Society for Quality (ASQ) Software Division
IEEE Reliability Society
Software Technology Support Center (STSC) 

The complete TCS2001 program can be explored on the Conference web site:
<http://www.uspdi.org/conference>

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ 

CONFERENCE FEATURES

* Pre-Conference (June 18-19) Workshops (2 days) and Tutorials (1 day)
will present comprehensive technical sessions for software testing
practitioners. 

Detailed descriptions of the Workshops/Tutorials can be checked out: 
<http://www.uspdi.org/conference/workshops.html> 

* Six General Sessions present Keynote Speakers.

* Thirty-two Concurrent Sessions divided into four parallel tracks present
a wide variety of topics and issues in major areas of: Management,
Automation, Technology, Requirements, Practices, Web and Case Studies. 

For detailed descriptions of the Technical Program, including Keynote
speakers see:
<http://www.uspdi.org/conference/program.html> 

* Vendor Exhibit of software testing tools and services.

* The Conference Volume (including the presentations and proceedings 
of the sessions) will be a valuable reference for testing projects.

* Networking:  
  - Birds-of-a-Feather Sessions allow you to meet and network with the
    speakers and other conference attendees, and discuss areas of mutual
    interest.
  - Lunch Discussion Groups will facilitate the exploration of common
    interest topics among the participants.

                              * * * * *

For complete program details and registration please contact the U.S.
Professional Development Institute (USPDI):

Regular Mail: USPDI, 612 Ethan Allen Avenue, Suite 100; Takoma Park,
              MD 20912-5400, USA.
Phone:        (+1) 301-270-1033
Fax:          (+1) 301-270-1040
E-mail:       admin@uspdi.org (Subject: TCS2001)

On-line:
Brochure request: <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/brochure.html> 
Registration:     <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/register.html>
Web download:     <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/printbrochure.html>

                               * * * * *
```

## Thread: Help — 27 this month / 29 total

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help

- **From:** "Eric Lavoie" <lavoie.eric@uqam.ca>
- **Date:** 2001-04-25T17:07:12-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<10HF6.3750$xA4.1166587@weber.videotron.net>`

```
Can some one tell me hour can a put month into days.  Because we have to do
a bill but we only have  the month.  We dont know to put month into days.

Anik

Sorry for me english
```

#### ↳ Re: Help

- **From:** Steve Thompson <sthompson_2@neo.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-25T21:43:19+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE7448A.ADB8EADE@neo.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<10HF6.3750$xA4.1166587@weber.videotron.net>`

```
Eric Lavoie wrote:
> 
> Can some one tell me hour can a put month into days.  Because we have to do
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> Sorry for me english

[Well, this is a test to see if I can post to news groups again.
There is no test like production!]

I am unsure what your question really is. Are you trying to
format a date?
The US version is MM/DD/YY or MM/DD/YYYY where MM=month DD=day
YY/YYYY=year (2 or 4 digit).

Now, if you are doing European style dates, then it is DD/MM/YY
or DD/MM/YYYY.

There is yet another date format that is unambiguous --
DDMMMYY/YYYY 
Where DD=day, MMM=three letter abbreviation for the month, and
YY/YYYY is the year (2 or 4 digit format).

Ok, if you are trying to determine how many days there are in a
month, well it depends. I must assume you understand the calendar
on the wall, and so you know that months vary between 28/29
(February non-leap year/leap year), and 30-31 (all the other 11
months).

If you are talking about the number of days in a "man month",
this is generally accepted to be 180 hrs (20 days * 8 hrs).

So, there are any number of answers you can get for the question
that you have asked. You need to be a little more precise.
```

- **From:** Susan Allen <susan.a.allen@boeing.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-25T22:30:05+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

Eric Lavoie wrote:
> 
> Can some one tell me hour can a put month into days.  Because we have to do
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> Sorry for me english

Is this a homework assignment?
You should know (for yourself) how many days each month has in a
standard year and a leap year.

There are many ways to calculate dates, Julian Date, Calendar Dates and
customized dates specific to an application- such as Manufacturing dates
(that exclude weekends and holidays).

	Susan A
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help

- **From:** Susan Allen <susan.a.allen@boeing.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-26T19:39:03+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE87957.8DDB917A@boeing.com>`
- **References:** `<10HF6.3750$xA4.1166587@weber.videotron.net> <3AE74FED.8EE47C78@boeing.com> <FPIF6.4104$xA4.1220486@weber.videotron.net>`

```
Anik Buzzell wrote:
> 
> Yes it is for an project and I am very bad in programming.  Do you know
> someone who did a electricity bill program.?

we do not encourage students to copy programs, you need to learn
yourself how to structure it.

Look over your requirements (the assignment)
Write down how the data needs to process (the flow for each record)
Determine what you know about each record
Determine what you need to find out from each record (which fields
trigger an action)
determine what the action(s) should be (write, change a field, ignore
the record, etc.)

when you have the mental model completed, then structure your program to
do the same thing.

Programming is very logical and very sequential at this stage of your
learning


	regards		Susan
```

Abbot and Costello did a routine, it went something like this:

01  who.

01 what
    05 second pic x(20).
01  where
    05  third   pic x(20).

I forget the rest of the routine, i was kind of young at the time but this may
help:

If who <= First
   Then who is on second?

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** aflinsch <avflinsch@att.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-27T11:37:09-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE99224.501DAEA@att.net>`
- **References:** `<3AE7F887.45CC155A@Azonic.co.nz> <20010425193838.27718.00000932@ng-bj1.aol.com> <zfJF6.4124$xA4.1238607@weber.videotron.net>`

```
Anik Buzzell wrote:
> 
> What do you meen Merhottin
> "Merhottin" <merhottin@aol.com> a ï¿½crit dans le message news:
> 20010425193838.27718.00000932@ng-bj1.aol.com...
> > This is better then an abbot and costello routine........

He means this --
Abbott: Alright, now whaddya want?

Costello: Now look, I'm the head of the sports department. I gotta
know the baseball players' names. Do you know the
guys' names?

Abbott: Oh sure.

Costello: So you go ahead and tell me some of their names.

Abbott: Well, I'll introduce you to the boys. You know sometimes
nowadays they give ballplayers peculiar names.

Costello: You mean funny names.

Abbott: Nicknames, pet names, like Dizzy Dean

Costello: His brother Daffy

Abbott: Daffy Dean

Costello: And their cousin!

Abbott: Who's that?

Costello: Goofy!

Abbott: Goofy, huh? Now let's see. We have on the bags - we have Who's
on first, What's on second, I Don't Know's on
third.

Costello: That's what I wanna find out.

Abbott: I say Who's on first, What's on second, I Don't Know's on
third

Costello: You know the fellows' names?

Abbott: Certainly!

Costello: Well then who's on first?

Abbott: Yes!

Costello: I mean the fellow's name!

Abbott: Who!

Costello: The guy on first!

Abbott: Who!

Costello: The first baseman!

Abbott: Who!

Costello: The guy playing first!

Abbott: Who is on first!

Costello: Now whaddya askin' me for?

Abbott: I'm telling you Who is on first.

Costello: Well, I'm asking YOU who's on first!

Abbott: That's the man's name.

Costello: That's who's name?

Abbott: Yes.

Costello: Well go ahead and tell me.

Abbott: Who.

Costello: The guy on first.

Abbott: Who!

Costello: The first baseman.

Abbott: Who is on first!

Costello: Have you got a contract with the first baseman?

Abbott: Absolutely.

Costello: Who signs the contract?

Abbott: Well, naturally!

Costello: When you pay off the first baseman every month, who gets the
money?

Abbott: Every dollar. Why not? The man's entitled to it.

Costello: Who is?

Abbott: Yes. Sometimes his wife comes down and collects it.

Costello: Who's wife?

Abbott: Yes.

Costello: All I'm tryin' to find out is what's the guy's name on first
base.

Abbott: Oh, no - wait a minute, don't switch 'em around. What is on
second base.

Costello: I'm not askin' you who's on second.

Abbott: Who is on first.

Costello: I don't know.

Abbott: He's on third - now we're not talkin' 'bout him.

Costello: Now, how did I get on third base?

Abbott: You mentioned his name!

Costello: If I mentioned the third baseman's name, who did I say is
playing third?

Abbott: No - Who's playing first.

Costello: Never mind first - I wanna know what's the guy's name on
third.

Abbott: No - What's on second.

Costello: I'm not askin' you who's on second.

Abbott: Who's on first.

Costello: I don't know.

Abbott: He's on third.

Costello: Aaah! Would you please stay on third base and don't go off
it?

Abbott: What was it you wanted?

Costello: Now who's playin' third base?

Abbott: Now why do you insist on putting Who on third base?

Costello: Why? Who am I putting over there?

Abbott: Yes. But we don't want him there.

Costello: What's the guy's name on third base?

Abbott: What belongs on second.

Costello: I'm not askin' you who's on second.

Abbott: Who's on first.

Costello: I don't know.

Abbott & Costello: THIRD BASE!

Costello: You got an outfield?

Abbott: Oh yes!

Costello: The left fielder's name?

Abbott: Why.

Costello: I don't know, I just thought I'd ask you.

Abbott: Well, I just thought I'd tell you.

Costello: Alright, then tell me who's playin' left field.

Abbott: Who is playing fir-

Costello: STAY OUTTA THE INFIELD! I wanna know what's the left
fielder's name.

Abbott: What's on second.

Costello: I'm not askin' you who's on second.

Abbott: Who's on first.

Costello: I don't know.

Abbott & Costello: THIRD BASE!

Costello: The left fielder's name?

Abbott: Why.

Costello: Because!

Abbott: Oh, he's center field.

Costello: Look, you gotta pitcher on this team?

Abbott: Now wouldn't this be a fine team without a pitcher.

Costello: The pitcher's name.

Abbott: Tomorrow.

Costello: You don't wanna tell me today?

Abbott: I'm tellin' you now.

Costello: Then go ahead.

Abbott: Tomorrow.

Costello: What time?

Abbott: What time what?

Costello: What time tomorrow are you going to tell me who's pitching?

Abbott: Now listen. Who is not pitching. Who is on fir-

Costello: I'll break your arm if you say Who's on first. I wanna know
what's the pitcher's name.

Abbott: What's on second.

Costello: I don't know.

Abbott & Costello: THIRD BASE!

Costello: You got a catcher?

Abbott: Oh, absolutely.

Costello: The catcher's name.

Abbott: Today.

Costello: Today. And Tomorrow's pitching.

Abbott: Now you've got it.

Costello: All we've got is a couple of days on the team.

Abbott: Well, I can't help that.

Costello: Well, I'm a catcher too.

Abbott: I know that.

Costello: Now suppose that I'm catching, Tomorrow's pitching on my
team and their heavy hitter gets up.

Abbott: Yes.

Costello: Tomorrow throws the ball. The batter bunts the ball. When he
bunts the ball, me being a good catcher, I wanna
throw the guy out at first base. So I pick up the ball and throw it to
who?

Abbott: Now that's the first thing you've said right.

Costello: I don't even know what I'm talkin' about!

Abbott: Well, that's all you have to do.

Costello: Is to throw the ball to first base.

Abbott: Yes.

Costello: Now who's got it?

Abbott: Naturally!

Costello: If I throw the ball to first base, somebody's gotta catch
it. Now who caught it?

Abbott: Naturally!

Costello: Who caught it?

Abbott: Naturally.

Costello: Who?

Abbott: Naturally!

Costello: Naturally.

Abbott: Yes.

Costello: So I pick up the ball and I throw it to Naturally.

Abbott: NO, NO, NO! You throw the ball to first base and Who gets it?

Costello: Naturally.

Abbott: That's right. There we go.

Costello: So I pick up the ball and I throw it to Naturally.

Abbott: You don't!

Costello: I throw it to who?

Abbott: Naturally.

Costello: THAT'S WHAT I'M SAYING!

Abbott: You're not saying it that way.

Costello: I said I throw the ball to Naturally.

Abbott: You don't - you throw the ball to Who?

Costello: Naturally!

Abbott: Well, say that!

Costello: THAT'S WHAT I'M SAYING! I throw the ball to who?

Abbott: Naturally.

Costello: Ask me.

Abbott: You throw the ball to Who?

Costello: Naturally.

Abbott: That's it.

Costello: SAME AS YOU!! I throw the ball to first base and who gets
it?

Abbott: Naturally!

Costello: Who has it?

Abbott: Naturally!

Costello: HE BETTER HAVE IT! I throw the ball to first base. Whoever
it is grabs the ball, so the guy runs to second.
Who picks up the ball and throws it to What, What throws it to I Don't
Know, I Don't Know throws it back to Tomorrow
- triple play.

Abbott: Yes.

Costello: Another guy gets up - it's a long fly ball to Because. Why?
I don't know. He's on third and I don't give a darn!

Abbott: What was that?


Costello: I said I don't give a darn!


Abbott: Oh, that's our shortstop.
```

All the rest I can't remember.
The calender hangs upon the wall,

<a id="t-9442fa6d9c"></a>
## Thread: Help: loading an array/table question — 4 this month / 5 total

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help: loading an array/table question

- **From:** goble@gtech (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 2001-04-26T00:21:30+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae7669e.3418093@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```
Hi all;

If I have a record like

01 rec.
	05 rname   pic x
	05 rscore1 pic 9
	05 rscore2 pic 9
	05 rscore3 pic 9
	05 rscore4 pic 9

and a table/array

01 table occurs X times.
	05 tname pic x.
	05 scores occurs 4 times.
		10 tscore pic 9.

I can then load the table with;

	perform load varying C from 1 by 1 until C=X.
load
	move rname to tname(C).
	move rscore1 to tscore(C, 1).
	move rscore2 to tscore(C, 2).
	move rscore3 to tscore(C, 3).
	move rscore4 to tscore(C, 4)

My question is can I have instead a simpler record like;

01 rec.
	05 rname   pic x
	05 rscores pic 9(04).

and a simpler load to load the table like with a single move like;

load
	move rname to tname(C).
	move rscores to scores(C).

I tried the above but the compiler (Microfocus personal) complained.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help: loading an array/table question

- **From:** Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-26T06:50:17-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AE81989.BCB6A8DA@brazee.net>`
- **References:** `<3ae7669e.3418093@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```


"David. E. Goble" wrote:

> Hi all;
>
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
>                 10 tscore pic 9.

I'm surprised your compiler accepts this.  Most don't, but I have used
at least one which does.


> I can then load the table with;
>
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
> I tried the above but the compiler (Microfocus personal) complained.

When you make a post such as this, tell us HOW it complained.  What did
it say?
Why is your RSCORES PIC 9(04) instead of PIC X(04)?
What does SCORES look like?
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help: loading an array/table question

- **From:** goble@gtech (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 2001-04-27T23:33:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ae89782.5444982@news.adelaide.on.net>`
- **References:** `<3ae7669e.3418093@news.adelaide.on.net> <3AE81989.BCB6A8DA@brazee.net>`

```
On Thu, 26 Apr 2001 06:50:17 -0600, Howard Brazee <howard@brazee.net>
wrote:
>
>When you make a post such as this, tell us HOW it complained.  What did
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Hi Howard;

Ok, here is what I have that works;

SELECT f100-matches-file
   ASSIGN TO DISK "constant/Matches.mst"
   ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL
   ACCESS IS SEQUENTIAL
   FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.

FD  f100-matches-file
   LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD
   RECORD CONTAINS 66 CHARACTERS
   DATA RECORD IS f100-matches-rec.
01 f100-matches-rec.
   05 f110-matches-match               Pic x(29).
   05 f120-grades-gradem               Pic 9(03).
   05 f130-grades-gradea               Pic 9(03).
   05 f140-grades-gradeb               Pic 9(03).
   05 f150-grades-gradec               Pic 9(03).
   05 f160-match-cal177                Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal22s                Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal22l                Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal32                 Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal38                 Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal357                Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal9mm                Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal44                 Pic 9.
   05 f160-match-cal45                 Pic 9.
   05 f170-match-stages                Pic 9.
   05 f171-mstage1-max                 Pic 9(03).
   05 f172-mstage2-max                 Pic 9(03).
   05 f173-mstage3-max                 Pic 9(03).
   05 f174-mstage4-max                 Pic 9(03).
   05 f180-matches-max                 Pic 9(03).

01 w700-matches-table.
   05 w705-match-sub                   Pic 9(02).
   05 w710-by-match OCCURS 16 TIMES INDEXED BY w710-match-inx.
      10 w711-match                   Pic x(29).
      10 w711a-stages                 Pic 9(01).
      10 w711d-out-of                 Pic 9(03).
      10 w712-by-grade OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY w912-grade-inx.
         15 w712b-grade              Pic 9(03).
      10 w713-match-calibra OCCURS 10 TIMES INDEXED BY w713-cal-inx.
         15 w713a-cal                Pic 9.
         15 w713b-cal-type           Pic x(03).
      10 w714-per-stage OCCURS 4 TIMES INDEXED BY w714-per-inx.
         15 w714a-stage-max          Pic 9(03).

  PERFORM 141000-LOAD-MATCH-ARRAY
     VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
     UNTIL w710-match-inx>15 OR w111-eof.
  PERFORM 141110-LOAD-CALIBRA-ARRAY
     VARYING w713-cal-inx FROM 1 BY 1
     UNTIL w713-cal-inx>9 OR w111-eof.
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  PERFORM 142000-ADD-TO-ARRAY.
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
141000-LOAD-MATCH-ARRAY.
*========================
READ f100-matches-file
   AT END
      MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
   NOT AT END
      PERFORM 141100-LOAD-MATCH
   END-READ.
141100-LOAD-MATCH.
*==================
   MOVE f110-matches-match TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
   MOVE f180-matches-max TO w711d-out-of(w710-match-inx).
*
* what I want to do is remove the explicit indexs
*
* to some thing like;
*	MOVE f120-grades (pic 9(12) TO w7xx-grades(w710-match-inx) 
* ...etc
*
   MOVE f120-grades-gradem TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 1).
   MOVE f130-grades-gradea TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 2).
   MOVE f140-grades-gradeb TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 3).
   MOVE f150-grades-gradec TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 4).
   MOVE f160-match-cal177 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 1).
   MOVE f160-match-cal22s TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 2).
   MOVE f160-match-cal22l TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 3).
   MOVE f160-match-cal32 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 4).
   MOVE f160-match-cal38 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 5).
   MOVE f160-match-cal357 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 6).
   MOVE f160-match-cal9mm TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 7).
   MOVE f160-match-cal44 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 8).
   MOVE f160-match-cal45 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 9).
   MOVE f170-match-stages TO w711a-stages(w710-match-inx).
   MOVE f171-mstage1-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 1).
   MOVE f172-mstage2-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 2).
   MOVE f173-mstage3-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 3).
   MOVE f174-mstage4-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 4).
   MOVE "PRA" TO w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, 10).
   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
141110-LOAD-CALIBRA-ARRAY.
*==========================
READ f500-calibras-file
  AT END
    MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
  NOT AT END
    PERFORM 141111-LOAD-CALIBRA
       VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
       UNTIL w710-match-inx>15
     MOVE "PRA" TO w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, w713-cal-inx)
  END-READ.
141111-LOAD-CALIBRA.
*====================
MOVE f510-calibra TO w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, w713-cal-inx).
142000-ADD-TO-ARRAY.
*====================
   MOVE "All Matches" TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
```

Hi David,

I think if you define your table like this, what you propose should work. 

01 table occurs X times.
05 tname pic x.
05 scores.
10 tscore occurs 4 times pic 9.

Regards, Jack

### SR. COBOL/CICS PROGRAMMERS - EXCELLENT COMPANY/OPPORTUNITY

- **From:** "Donna Nixon" <dm-nixon@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-26T16:17:18+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** biz.comp.jobs.offered,biz.comp.software,can.jobs.ont.jobs,comp.job.offered,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<iSXF6.4851$HF.383128@news4.rdc1.on.home.com>`

```
REFERENCE NO:  353ITJ/l460/0l

DATE:  April 26/0l

LOCATION:  Toronto

JOB TITLE:  Sr Cobol/Cics/Ingenium Programmers
(Multiple Positions Available) - Contract & Full-time

technical teams (for senior positions), task estimation and scopt
determination, provide support for clients.

ingenium/capsil, good communication

## Thread: Off topic — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Off-topic and spam`](topics.md#spam)_

### Off topic

- **From:** Andy <andy@whoofmail.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-27T21:30:04
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<tejp6s9m7pg703@corp.supernews.com>`

```
Hi
I am relatively new to internet. I want to ask a stupid offtopic question.
Recently there has been lots of post going on regarding downloading 
*that* "Evidence Eliminator software" in almost all newsgroups.

My confusion:
Usually I delete the files from:
step 1. in explorer tools>options
        C:\WINDOWS\Temporary Internet Files (including delete all offline 
content)
step 2. I do clear history by pressing clear history button.
step 3. I go into general tab>settings>view files>and delete all (cookies) 
by selecting all files.
step 4. Also i go into content tab>Auto complete>clear forms + passwords

Now my question is "How the hell can any one view the pages I visited, 
after doing the above procedure?" Is this not the so called cache which i 
have already deleted?
And if someone can still view the visited pages or web addresses or any 
thing at all i would like to know HOW please. I am very curious about 
this. Please prove me wrong I know very little yet.
Sorry for posting this to comp.lang.cobol the reason being i am a cobol 
student and this is the only newsgroup i use it more frequently.

Thanks for sharing
Andy
```

#### ↳ Re: Off topic

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald_tees@sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2001-04-27T21:44:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<pLlG6.651808$f36.18618281@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<tejp6s9m7pg703@corp.supernews.com>`

```

"Andy" <andy@whoofmail.com> wrote in message
news:tejp6s9m7pg703@corp.supernews.com...
> Hi
> I am relatively new to internet. I want to ask a stupid offtopic question.
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Deleting a file does not delete the file.  It deletes the entry in the file
index, then returns the block numbers to the available space manager for the
OS.  If you want to scan through unused disk space, you can still see the
data that was in the files, though finding it and putting it back into a
semblance of order could be a major undertaking.  A "secure" erase writes
over top of the pages before erasing them.

Be that as it may, you have to be a terminal paranoia case (or really
stupid) to buy that software.  It is a scam, feeding on people's ignorance.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Off topic

>

…[37 more quoted lines elided]…
> stupid) to buy that software.  It is a scam, feeding on people's ignorance.

Or feeding on their guilt.  If it was as easy to delete evidence as many people think, 
the police would not be as successful as they are in retrieving evidence from seized 
computers. A forensic package called "EnCase" (worth about $7,000 I think) or something 
of the sort can search the hard drive and find all sorts of stuff that Windows has 
tucked away and that you didn't know were there.  It can also reassemble bits of files 
including image files in what are supposed to be deleted clusters and produce an audit 
trail for evidentiary purposes.

One person who should know believes it to be the best selling shareware program in the 
world right now. I wondered why the FBI/CIA/Surete/Scotland Yard had not seized a list 
of the buyers.  I asked the publisher.  "Because we're incorporated in Hong Kong".
```

<a id="t-444b383f65"></a>
## Thread: Cobol as a structured language — 8 this month / 76 total

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### Cobol as a structured language

- **From:** Jerry@Westrick.com
- **Date:** 2001-04-28T14:28:41+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20010428.14284100@george.local>`

```
Hello Everybody.

First off, I'm not a newbie (over 25 years expirience 
programming in over a dozen languages).

Second, I've been quote as saying "Cobol/CICS" is my mother tongue"! 
(Since my first major application (1979) was writen in it).
So I'm not some PC convert flaming Cobol.  

I've been lurking in comp.lang.cobol and alt.cobol for a couple of 
weeks now.  In anticipation of a contract to convert a banking 
application from SCO, MicroFocus cobol, to either Linux MF Cobol 
or Solaris MF Cobol (meriant).  Meriant don't seem to support the
newer versions of Linux, so my client is tending toward Solaris.

Having said all that, here is my 2 bits:

1) Cobol can be written in such a way as to be extreemly readable, 
   and thereby extreemly maintainable.  When done so it is (in my
   opinion) THE most maintainable language I've ever used.

2) Cobol can be written in such a way as to be the extreemly unreadable,
   and thereby extreemly unmaintainable.  When done so it is close
   to the least maintainable language I've ever used.

Sound like a contradiction? Well that's my expirience.

Of course my expirience is with the older dialects of the language.
The problem, I find, in Cobol is that the Cobol language is flat,
and not hierarchical.  By this I mean that Cobol all the data and code
is global, and thereby globaly accessable.

I remember my first Cobol program, in 1977.  It brought the IBM COBOL 
compiler
crashing down (with an ABEND).  After multiple reviews of the code I was 
unable
to find the problem.  I went to the local COBOL expert (Daddy) for help.
His comment was "I wish this would compile, but ...".  Here is what I was 
trying to do:

Data Division.
  01 Rec01.
     05  Rec01-Fld01     pic ...
     05  Rec01-Fld02     pic ...
     05  Rec01-Fld03     pic ...

Procedure Division.
  Init-Rec01.
     Move ... to Rec01-Fld01.
     Move ... to Rec01-Fld02.
     Move ... to Rec01-Fld03.

Data Division.
  01 Rec02.
     05  Rec02-Fld01     pic ...
     05  Rec02-Fld02     pic ...
     05  Rec02-Fld03     pic ...

Procedure Division.
  Init-Rec02.
     Move ... to Rec02-Fld01.
     Move ... to Rec02-Fld02.
     Move ... to Rec02-Fld03.

As a new COBOL programmer I did not see any reason that the data 
definitions 
could not be defined near the code that used them!  And all the examples 
had
short little Data Divisions and short little Procedure Devisions.

Cobol programs which exceed a certain size grow into monstrous 
maintenance 
nightmares.  This is forced onto the programmer by the language itself, 
since 
it does not allow for any type of local variables, or local procedures.  
To
get around this, I started using the language in a style which was soon 
dubbed
"CALLBOL".  Spreading the Code into different compilable units.  Most of 
you
know all about the problems this creates.

Taking examples of good things from other languages:
In "C" (and many other languages), I can define variables local to a 
procedure.
Please don't misunderstand me. Yes, I can "C", but I'd rather be blind!  
But I
could really use variables defined local to a paragraph in Cobol.  

Additionally, the field names in the above examples composed of record 
name 
dash field name ie Rec01-Fld03.  This naming convention is widely used to 
offer
the programmer an idea of what is being done as shown below.

    Move ... to Rec01-Fld03.

Is read as "Move something to Fld03 of Rec01".  Hmmmm, when COBOL is 
supposed to 
be read like english the why don't we just say

    Move ... to Fld03 of Rec01.

Of course, this would get quite windy

    Move 20 to day                  of birth-date of Employee-Rec.
    Move 03 to month                of birth-date of Employee-Rec.
    Move 19 to century of full-year of birth-date of Employee-Rec.
    Move 59 to year    of full-year of birth-date of Employee-Rec.
    ....

But that could be simplified with a trick out of pascal to:

    with birth-date of Employee-Rec
    begin
      Move 20 to day                    
      Move 03 to month                  
      Move 19 to century of full-year
      Move 59 to year    of full-year
    end.

Although I think the first version is easier to read and therefore more
maintainable, the second form would surely come in handy sometimes.

In my opinion only 01 level definitions should be directly accessable
and all fields accessable only in this way!


My Question:

What do the rest of the COBOL users out there think?  What do the newer
dialects offer in this hierarchical-structuring aspect?  Is anybody 
working
on something in this direction?



Jerry Westrick
```

i think it'd create more problems than it would solve. everytime you

just out of high school cobol programmer who before wrote using 02
variables will maintain a program, and it'll fail, and only the
compiler writers or this newsgroup will know why.

actually, when i was just starting out cobol, i'd often use level 2 or
lower variables instead of 88, because i wouldn't know if 88 would be

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol as a structured language

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T17:32:55+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AED93B7.88AC8F0A@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<20010428.14284100@george.local> <dt8jetghqk2bgvdlv8laulqbh9vmdjl5lc@4ax.com>`

```
> >But that could be simplified with a trick out of pascal to:
> >
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> >    end.

The 'Pascal trick' is not as useful as it may appear on a superficial
view.

Real programs are more likely to not use literals (which in this case
would require a program rewrite for each employee) but would use, say,
transaction data or a block of input data.  This then requires:

      MOVE Input-Birth-Date-Day     TO Employee-Birth-Date-Day
      etc

This would require WITH Birth-Date OF Input-Block as well as WITH
Birth-Date OF Employee and then the 
      MOVE Day     TO Day     
is ambiguous.

The 'Pascal trick' is more useful for teaching programs than real ones.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol as a structured language

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-28T17:41:50+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AEB0100.AF67F2B5@home.com>`
- **References:** `<20010428.14284100@george.local>`

```


Jerry@Westrick.com wrote:

> Hello Everybody.
>
> First off, I'm not a newbie (over 25 years expirience
> programming in over a dozen languages).

Well,  this is not a complete answer to your questions. But take one specific
area, where you would prefer to have data 'localized'. This is already
available within the PC compilers from Fujitsu and Merant(Net Express) using
OO methods - Local-storage section. Even within Structured for COBOL 200X -
Local-storage section will also be a feature, plus coupled with User Defined
Functions, (mini routines).

You're much too late for any changes to be made to COBOL 200X - but - if you
think there are good arguments for some of the points you are suggesting, you
can always submit a paper to J4 for future consideration.

Jimmy
```

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

…[3 more quoted lines elided]…

## Thread: printer — 2 msgs

### printer

- **From:** "[PHH]Apollo" <swartenbroekxro@wanadoo.be>
- **Date:** 2001-04-28T18:53:38+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9cesi6$1cu1$1@scavenger.euro.net>`

```
How do you print with a standard cobol 85 compiler (Micro Focus 2.0) to a
printer connected to lpt1?
```

#### ↳ Re: printer

- **From:** lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca
- **Date:** 2001-04-28T17:49:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-dPfkLjWVKgo7@24-108-102-82.ivideon.com>`
- **References:** `<9cesi6$1cu1$1@scavenger.euro.net>`

```
On Sat, 28 Apr 2001 16:53:38, "[PHH]Apollo" 
<swartenbroekxro@wanadoo.be> wrote:

> How do you print with a standard cobol 85 compiler (Micro Focus 2.0) to a
> printer connected to lpt1?
> 
> 

Specify a file assignment to "lpt1:"

Specify the file as "organization line sequential"

open it extend or output

write to the file

close the file

---- selected code bits ------------

select out-file assign "lpt1:"
organization line sequential.

FD out-file.
01  a-record-name.
02  filler pic x(200)


open extend out-file
move "some stuff to print" to a-record-name
write a-record-name
close out-file

------ end code btis -------------

Good luck
```

---

Said That
You Send $1.00 To Each Of The 6 Names And Addresses Stated In The
Article.
You Then Place Your Own Name And Address At The Bottom Of The List
At #6, And Post The Article To At Least 200 Newsgroups.
(There Are Thousands). No Catch, That Was It. So After Thinking
It Over And Talking To A Few People At First, I Thought About Trying
It.
I Figured "What Have I Got To Lose Except 6 Stamps And $6.00,
Right?".
Then, I Invested The Measly $6.00.

Well, GUESS WHAT!!!! Within 7 Days,
I Started Getting Money In The Mail!!!
I Was Shocked. I Figured It Would End Soon, But The Money
Just Kept Coming In. In My First Week, I Made About $25.00.
By The End Of The Second Week,I Had Made A Total Of Over $1000.00!!
In The Third Week, I Had Over $10,000 And Its Still Growing. This Is
Now My
Forth Week And I Have Made A Total Of Just Over $42,000 And Its Still
Coming
In Rapidly. Its Certainly Worth $6.00 And 6 Stamps, I've Spent More
Than
That On The Lottery!!

## Thread: Cheap Ansi standard Cobol for single user. — 4 this month / 13 total

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### Cheap Ansi standard Cobol for single user.

- **From:** tornado@totalserve.co.uk
- **Date:** 2001-04-29T01:26:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3aeb6c63.4692762@news.totalserve.net>`

```
Hi all,

I'm new to this group but I've been using Realia Cobol which isn't ansi
standard and Microfocus Cobol for Dos which is real expensive for a single
developer. I am now unemployed but want to use my experience to start
developing on my own.

I want to develop Multi-user applications but din't have the financial
resources to pay megabucks for the packages I've seen on the market recently.

Does anyone have any suggestions?

Regards,
Tornado.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cheap Ansi standard Cobol for single user.

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-29T02:23:49+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AEB7B59.636FA341@home.com>`
- **References:** `<3aeb6c63.4692762@news.totalserve.net>`

```


tornado@totalserve.co.uk wrote:

> Hi all,
>
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
> Tornado.

Two possibilities as you are short of funds :-

    (a) Fujitsu - certainly not latest version, but a copy comes with Thane
Hubbell's
        "Teach Yourself COBOL in 24 Hours". But also check out Fujitsu

    (b) Merant(Micro Focus) Check their site or objectz.com and see if you can
get a
    copy of Net Express - University Edition.

Both will get you up and running with latest enhancements, (include COBOL '97 and
each vendor's respective 'extensions' beyond that date); and you could also take
a dabble at OO.

Go to cobolreport.com and check out Bill Klein's COBOL FAQ.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

```

## Thread: "P" picture character — 20 this month / 31 total

### "P" picture character

- **From:** "Jude Robert" <juderobert1@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-29T21:31:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com>`

```
Hello,

I have been working with COBOL for 15+ years and in all that time  I
have never used the 'P' picture character nor have I ever seen it used
in any program I have supported. And during those years I have seen and
supported many programs.

I was just wondering if anyone of you have ever used or seen it used.

Thanks!


(I guess I have too much time on my hands this weekend to be thinking of
this)
```

- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com>`

```
15 years here as well and never seen a 'P' in the pic clause. What
does it do ?

"Jude Robert" <juderobert1@home.com> wrote in message
news:2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com...
> Hello,
>
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: "P" picture character

- **From:** "Rufio" <davecawdell@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-29T23:20:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<el1H6.59643$181.9230589@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`
- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com> <9ci314$t9f$1@newsg1.svr.pol.co.uk>`

```
As per my VSII manual -

P - an assumed decimal scaling position. It is used to specify the
location of an assumed decimal point, when the point is not within
the number that appears in the data item.

e.g. PIC PPP999 would have a range of valid values from 0 to .000999.

In my 14 years - I've never used it either - normally use a separate scaling
factor.

Wonder how well it relates to other numeric & edited fields e.g. ZZZ999.

P.S. is PIC VZZZ999 a valid pic ?


"Alister Munro" <alister@specsoft.freeserve.co.uk> wrote in message
news:9ci314$t9f$1@newsg1.svr.pol.co.uk...
> 15 years here as well and never seen a 'P' in the pic clause. What
> does it do ?
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: "P" picture character

- **From:** "Jude Robert" <juderobert1@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T00:31:08+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<gn2H6.56596$JI6.323515@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com>`
- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com> <9ci314$t9f$1@newsg1.svr.pol.co.uk> <el1H6.59643$181.9230589@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```

"Rufio" <davecawdell@home.com> wrote in message
news:el1H6.59643$181.9230589@news1.rdc1.az.home.com...
> As per my VSII manual -
>
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> In my 14 years - I've never used it either - normally use a separate
scaling
> factor.
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

No. Only one type of picture character can define digits to the right of the
decimal point.

    .ZZZZZ is valid
    VZZZZ is valid.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: "P" picture character: Solution

- **From:** "Apollo" <swartenbroekxro@wanadoo.be>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T10:14:40+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9cj6t0$14c$1@scavenger.euro.net>`
- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com> <9ci314$t9f$1@newsg1.svr.pol.co.uk> <el1H6.59643$181.9230589@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```

Rufio <davecawdell@home.com> schreef in berichtnieuws
el1H6.59643$181.9230589@news1.rdc1.az.home.com...
> As per my VSII manual -
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> e.g. PIC PPP999 would have a range of valid values from 0 to .000999.

pic ppp999 is invalid
pic 999ppp would have a range of valid values from 0 to 999000 in steps of
1000.


> In my 14 years - I've never used it either - normally use a separate
scaling
> factor.
>
> Wonder how well it relates to other numeric & edited fields e.g. ZZZ999.

This is just an editing picture


> P.S. is PIC VZZZ999 a valid pic ?


No



> "Alister Munro" <alister@specsoft.freeserve.co.uk> wrote in message
> news:9ci314$t9f$1@newsg1.svr.pol.co.uk...
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> > > in any program I have supported. And during those years I have seen
and
> > > supported many programs.
> > >
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> > > (I guess I have too much time on my hands this weekend to be thinking
of
> > > this)
> > >

PPP999 is invalid
999PPP gives you a number like 679000. if you gives this a value you have to
give this value 679000 not 679. those P is handy when you use rounded

01    a        pic 999ppp.
01    b        pic 9999    value 4687.
01    c        pic 99999    value 21137.

compute a rounded = b + c.

the sum is 25824 but now a has the value 26000, without the rounded a would
have the value 25000.

You also can use P behind a comma: VPPP99,
V999PP is invalid

I'm studying cobol for the second year at school, never seen it at school,
but I bought a book of cobol, and there I saw it explained.
```

#### ↳ Re: "P" picture character

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T00:00:29+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<xW1H6.734$Rn1.435311@paloalto-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com>`

```
Jude Robert <juderobert1@home.com> wrote in message
news:2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com...
> Hello,
>
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks!

I wrote a record layout sizing utility. Never having used or seen scaled
decimals myself, I decided not to support scaled decimals. I knew it
existed, so I put right in the documentation that I did not support 'P' in
the PICTURE IS clause. (Although I actually did support it for dataname size
purposes, because I ignored it when parsing the PICTURE clause).

First week it was available (at www.flexus.com/softwaredownload.html, get
file COBFD.ZIP) someone dropped me a note saying they had to have it. I said
no.

I have a list of what I am going to support in the next version. Scaled
Decimals has still not made that list.

MCM
```

On Sun, 29 Apr 2001 21:31:42 GMT, "Jude Robert" <juderobert1@home.com> wrote:

>Hello,
>
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>this)

I've never seen it used, but I know of a good case when we *could* use it.  I
work for a bank.  We assign cash reserve limits in hundred dollar increments.
We actually have the D-RSRV-LIMIT field set as S9(7) COMP-3, taking up 4 bytes.
If we intead had it as S9(5)P(2) we could save a byte.

Wow, huh?

Frank Swarbrick
home: infocat@sprynet.com / work: frank.swarbrick@efirstbank.com
"I'm very seldom naughty." --Willow Rosenberg "Buffy the Vampire Slayer"
```

"Frank Swarbrick" <infocat@sprynet.com

>  We assign cash reserve limits in hundred dollar increments.

> We actually have the D-RSRV-LIMIT field set as S9(7) COMP-3, taking
up 4 bytes.

> If we intead had it as S9(5)P(2) we could save a byte.

A byte here, a byte there. Pretty soon you're talking about not very
much.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: "P" picture character

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** JD <pb@dazoo.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T18:48:02-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AEDEBA2.CC9B0C09@dazoo.com>`
- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com> <mmipetge8b3bpaccfent7kaco8qa6imfbn@4ax.com> <RYjH6.79935$vg5.4332537@news5.aus1.giganews.com> <ZDkH6.61856$181.9732254@news1.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```


Rufio wrote:

> "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com> wrote in message
> news:RYjH6.79935$vg5.4332537@news5.aus1.giganews.com...
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
> Seems kinda contradictory.

Not at all.  Your first example has to do with saving space, for example in
record layouts.

The second example, justifying (actually syncronizing) on the word or half-word
has to do with performance.  The number of instructions generated by the
compiler can be less when the comp fields are syncronized on the
word/half-word.  With today's big hummers, virtually no-one considers
performance issues like this.  Back when I was coding on a 64-K machine, it
really mattered.

>
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> >
```

#### ↳ Re: "P" picture character

- **From:** Arnold Trembley <arnold.trembley@worldnet.att.net>

Jude Robert wrote:
> 
> Hello,
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> I was just wondering if anyone of you have ever used or seen it used.

I've been coding COBOL for most of my nearly 22 years programming and
never seen it used either.

I've also never seen exponentiation used, or IBM COMP-1 and COMP-2
floating point.  I've hardly ever seen BLANK WHEN ZERO or JUSTIFY.

I did run into a problem converting OS/VS COBOL to COBOL II in the
early 1990's with the IBM NOTE verb.  I was trying to write a program
to convert them automagically, and NOTE was a problem.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: "P" picture character

- **From:** Peter Lacey <lacey@mb.sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T09:03:11-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AED709E.3B62AD74@mb.sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<2L%G6.56299$JI6.320659@news1.rdc1.tx.home.com> <3AECD737.B36F8369@worldnet.att.net>`

```
Arnold Trembley wrote:

> I've also never seen exponentiation used, or IBM COMP-1 and COMP-2
> floating point.  I've hardly ever seen BLANK WHEN ZERO or JUSTIFY.
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> http://arnold.trembley.home.att.net/

I have not used comp-1 or comp-2, and rarely exponentation (usually it's only
squaring a field and for that A * A is just as good and saves tons of object
code); but both BLANK WHEN ZERO and JUSTIFY save lots of programming on
reports: I have had many cases where our users wanted blanks on a report
instead of zeros.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: "P" picture character

- **From:** Pat Hall <phall@certcoinc.com>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T10:50:54-05:00

```
Arnold if you used COBOL Report writer you wold use 'Blank when zero' quite
often.  Otherwise probably seldom.  I have one program that uses comp-1 but
only to pass info from the mainframe to a flakey pc program..  I never even
knew 'p' existed.  Or maybe I did and my old and feeble mind tucked it away
jsut a bit to far to retrieve.  27 years of COBOL will do that to you<EG>

PatH

<a id="t-291c435e95"></a>
## Thread: Solve for this problem — 2 this month / 3 total

### Solve for this problem

- **From:** "Jarkko K���hk���nen" <jarkko.kahkonen@softageneraattori.fi>
- **Date:** 2001-04-30T07:00:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<J48H6.20$vh7.3071@read2.inet.fi>`
- **References:** `<0Yyy6.15$AX2.990@read2.inet.fi>`

```
Here is walkthrough for all my problems (this may help other people too. I
hope...):
Fujitsu Cobol compiler V3.0 wants that section names, statements and so on,
are in their right columns.
Columns 1-6 are for line numbers, column 7 is for special marks like *
(comment mark),
columns 8-11 are for beginning of section and paragraph names, columns 12-72
are for beginning of statements.

That's it! Get it?

Thanks to everybody!

Jake.

Sent: Wednesday, April 04, 2001 9:13 AM

make
